### 5.3 Accessibility Requirements

- **Keyboard Alternative:** Tab to train, Enter to pick up, Arrow keys to navigate routes, Enter to drop
- **Screen Reader:** "KS-012, fit status, 42,350 kilometers, PM1 due in 15 days. Press Enter to pick up."
- **ARIA Attributes:** `aria-grabbed`, `aria-dropeffect`, live region for drop confirmation
- **Touch:** Long-press (300ms) to initiate drag, haptic feedback on grab/drop

## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 9.1 Proximity

- **Fleet Overview:** Metrics grouped together in left sidebar
- **Train Cards:** Related data (ID, mileage, PM) tightly grouped
- **Recommendations:** Suggestion and alternatives in same panel

### 9.2 Similarity

- **Train Status:** Consistent color coding across all views
  - рҹҹў Green = FIT / Safe / Good
  - рҹҹЎ Yellow = RESTRICTED / Approaching / Fair
  - рҹ”ҙ Red = UNFIT / Overdue / Poor
- **Card Components:** Same border radius (8px), shadow (2px blur)

### 9.3 Enclosure

- **Route Slots:** Clear boundary for drop zones
- **Message Cards:** Distinct containers for each message
- **Panels:** Sidebar and main content clearly separated

### 9.4 Continuity

- **Weekly Grid:** Horizontal timeline reading left-to-right
- **Score Breakdown:** Vertical list with progress bars aligned
- **Fleet Balance:** Left-aligned labels, right-aligned values

### 9.5 Figure-Ground

- **Drag Preview:** Elevated shadow distinguishes dragged item
- **Active Route:** Highlighted background during assignment
- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention

### 9.6 Common Fate

- **Multi-Select:** Selected items move/highlight together
- **Route Groups:** Routes in same category collapse/expand together

---

## 12. Color System

### 12.1 Status Colors

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| FIT / Success | Green | #22C55E | FIT trains, successful assignments |
| RESTRICTED / Warning | Amber | #F59E0B | RESTRICTED trains, approaching PM |
| UNFIT / Error | Red | #EF4444 | UNFIT trains, failed assignments |
| Info / AI | Blue | #3B82F6 | AI suggestions, information |
| Neutral | Gray | #6B7280 | Inactive, disabled states |

### 12.2 Score Colors

| Score Range | Color | Label |
|-------------|-------|-------|
| 95-100 | #22C55E | Excellent |
| 80-94 | #84CC16 | Good |
| 60-79 | #F59E0B | Fair |
| 40-59 | #F97316 | Poor |
| 0-39 | #EF4444 | Unsuitable |

### 12.3 Priority Colors

| Priority | Color | Background |
|----------|-------|------------|
| Urgent | #DC2626 | #FEF2F2 |
| High | #F97316 | #FFF7ED |
| Normal | #3B82F6 | #EFF6FF |
| Low | #6B7280 | #F9FAFB |

---

## 13. Performance Requirements

### 13.1 Load Times

| Metric | Target | Maximum |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
| Route List Render | <500ms | 1s |
| Train Pool Load (128 trains) | <300ms | 500ms |
| AI Recommendation | <1s | 3s |
| Drag-and-Drop Response | <50ms | 100ms |

### 13.2 Optimization Strategies

1. **Virtual scrolling** for train pool (>20 items)
2. **Debounced search** (300ms delay)
3. **Skeleton loading** for async data
4. **Optimistic updates** for assignments
5. **WebSocket** for real-time status updates

---

## 14. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 14.1 Operational Challenges

1. **Multi-day assignments:** Can a train be pre-assigned for multiple days? How to handle PM interruptions?
2. **Split shifts:** Can one train serve multiple routes in a day? How to sequence?
3. **Depot constraints:** If train is at Depot A, can it serve route starting at Depot B?
4. **Crew dependencies:** Is crew assignment coupled with train assignment?
5. **Fleet mixing rules:** Can Kinki Sharyo and Alstom be assigned interchangeably to all routes?

### 14.2 UX Challenges

6. **Undo granularity:** Undo single assignment vs undo all changes?
7. **Auto-save vs manual save:** Should changes persist immediately or require explicit save?
8. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same day?
9. **Historical edits:** Can past assignments be modified? Audit trail?
10. **Template schedules:** Can users save assignment patterns as templates?

### 14.3 AI/Algorithm Challenges

11. **Score transparency:** How much algorithm detail to expose to users?
12. **Override learning:** Should the system learn from user overrides to improve future suggestions?
13. **Fairness constraints:** Should mileage be strictly balanced or allow temporary imbalances?
14. **Look-ahead horizon:** Should AI consider tomorrow's schedule when suggesting today?
15. **Constraint conflicts:** How to prioritize when fitness and mileage balance conflict?

### 14.4 Integration Challenges

16. **Maximo sync timing:** When are fitness statuses updated from Maximo?
17. **Real-time mileage:** Is actual mileage available in real-time or end-of-day?
18. **Schedule lock time:** When does a draft schedule become immutable?
19. **Notification triggers:** Which changes should trigger Ops-Maint messages automatically?
20. **Export format:** What format does the external scheduling system expect?

---

## 15. Sources & References

### 15.1 Competitor Research

- [CloudMoyo CCM](https://rail.cloudmoyo.com/crew-management-software-solution/) - Accept/reject via IVR and mobile
- [Tracsis TRACS](https://tracsisops.com/software/operations-planning-software/tracs-scheduling-software) - Long-term/short-term planning modes
- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations) - Automatic validation of route/traction knowledge
- [HASTUS (GIRO)](https://www.progressiverailroading.com/internet-digital/article/Software-providers-offer-ways-to-optimize-rail-crew-scheduling-rostering-and-recordkeeping--62872) - Work rules and fatigue management

### 15.2 UX Patterns

- [SAP Fiori AI Recommendations](https://www.sap.com/design-system/fiori-design-web/v1-120/foundations/ai-and-joule-design/guidelines/recommendations) - Human input wins, detect-explain-compare-act
- [Nielsen Norman Group: Drag and Drop](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/drag-drop/) - Ease of use guidelines
- [Smashing Magazine: Drag-Drop UX](https://smart-interface-design-patterns.com/articles/drag-and-drop-ux/) - Visual feedback states
- [Hicron Fleet Management Dashboard](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/) - Role-based personalization

### 15.3 Technical References

- [react-dnd](https://react-dnd.github.io/react-dnd/about) - React drag-and-drop library
- [dnd-kit](https://dndkit.com/) - Modern drag-and-drop toolkit
- [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/) - Animation library for React

---

## 16. Appendix: TypeScript Types Reference

See `src/mileage/types/run-assignment.ts` for complete type definitions:
- `Route` - Route/service definition
- `RunAssignment` - Single train-route-day assignment
- `DailySchedule` - All assignments for a day
- `TrainRecommendation` - AI recommendation with score breakdown
- `AssignmentOptimizationRequest` - Optimization input parameters
- `AssignmentOptimizationResult` - Optimized assignments with metrics
- `AssignmentWarning` - Warning types and severity
- `MileageDeviation` - Planned vs actual deviation tracking
- `FleetDailySummary` - Daily fleet status summary
- `OpsMaintenanceMessage` - Operations-Maintenance communication

---

   554вҶ’## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied
   555вҶ’
   556вҶ’### 8.1 Proximity
   557вҶ’
   558вҶ’- **KPI Cards:** Grouped in a single row for quick scanning
   559вҶ’- **Chart + Legend:** Legend positioned close to chart
   560вҶ’- **Table + Actions:** Filter controls adjacent to table
   561вҶ’
   562вҶ’### 8.2 Similarity
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’- **Status Colors:** Consistent across all views
   565вҶ’  - рҹҹў Green = Under budget / On target / Compliant
   566вҶ’  - рҹҹЎ Yellow = Warning / Approaching threshold
   567вҶ’  - рҹ”ҙ Red = Over budget / Overdue / Critical
   568вҶ’- **Card Design:** Same border radius, shadow, padding
   569вҶ’
   570вҶ’### 8.3 Enclosure
   571вҶ’
   572вҶ’- **Sections:** Clear card boundaries with subtle shadows
   573вҶ’- **Tables:** Header row visually distinct from data rows
   574вҶ’- **Charts:** Contained within labeled containers
   575вҶ’
   576вҶ’### 8.4 Continuity
   577вҶ’
   578вҶ’- **Trend Lines:** Smooth curves leading eye through time
   579вҶ’- **Waterfall:** Connected bars showing flow from budget to actual
   580вҶ’- **Progress Bars:** Horizontal continuity for completion status
   581вҶ’
   582вҶ’### 8.5 Figure-Ground
   583вҶ’
   584вҶ’- **Charts:** White background with colored data
   585вҶ’- **Alerts:** Elevated cards on lighter background
   586вҶ’- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention
   587вҶ’
   588вҶ’### 8.6 Common Fate
   589вҶ’
   590вҶ’- **Interactive Highlights:** Hovering chart point highlights related table row
   591вҶ’- **Filter Application:** All components update simultaneously
   592вҶ’
   593вҶ’---

   686вҶ’| Cost KPI | Cost Compliance tab |

   697вҶ’| CSV | Table data only | System integration |
   698вҶ’| Image | Individual charts | Reports/emails |
   699вҶ’

   701вҶ’
   702вҶ’## 12. Performance Requirements
   703вҶ’
   704вҶ’### 12.1 Load Times
   705вҶ’
   706вҶ’| Metric | Target | Maximum |
   707вҶ’|--------|--------|---------|
   708вҶ’| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
   709вҶ’| Tab Switch | <500ms | 1s |
   710вҶ’| Period Change | <1s | 2s |
   711вҶ’| Export Generation | <5s | 15s |
   712вҶ’
   713вҶ’### 12.2 Data Refresh
   714вҶ’
   715вҶ’| Data Type | Refresh Interval |
   716вҶ’|-----------|------------------|
   717вҶ’| Real-time KPIs | 5 minutes |
   718вҶ’| Charts/Trends | On demand |
   719вҶ’| Reports | Daily batch |
   720вҶ’| Alerts | Push notification |
   721вҶ’
   722вҶ’---
   723вҶ’
   724вҶ’## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   725вҶ’
   726вҶ’### 13.1 Business Logic
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’1. **Compliance aggregation:** How to weight sub-metrics into overall score?
   729вҶ’2. **Tolerance bands:** Different tolerances for different PM levels?
   730вҶ’3. **Cost allocation:** How are shared costs split between fleets?
   731вҶ’4. **Baseline adjustments:** When can budget baselines be updated?
   732вҶ’5. **Seasonality:** Are there seasonal patterns in mileage/cost compliance?
   733вҶ’
   734вҶ’### 13.2 UX Challenges
   735вҶ’
   736вҶ’6. **Alert fatigue:** How many alerts before users ignore them?
   737вҶ’7. **Drill-down depth:** How many levels before users get lost?
   738вҶ’8. **Comparison periods:** What comparisons are most valuable (MoM, YoY)?
   739вҶ’9. **Goal setting:** Who sets compliance targets and can they be adjusted?
   740вҶ’10. **Notification preferences:** Email vs in-app vs SMS for alerts?
   741вҶ’
   742вҶ’### 13.3 Technical Challenges
   743вҶ’
   744вҶ’11. **Historical data retention:** How far back to keep detailed data?
   745вҶ’12. **Data reconciliation:** Handling discrepancies between systems?
   746вҶ’13. **Offline access:** Do users need offline compliance reports?
   747вҶ’14. **Multi-currency:** Are there cross-border cost considerations?
   748вҶ’15. **Audit trail:** What compliance changes need to be logged?
   749вҶ’
   750вҶ’### 13.4 Integration
   751вҶ’
   752вҶ’16. **Maximo cost sync:** How are actual costs imported?
   753вҶ’17. **Budget import:** Source system for budget data?
   754вҶ’18. **External reporting:** Format for regulatory compliance reports?
   755вҶ’19. **Dashboard embedding:** Can dashboards be embedded elsewhere?
   756вҶ’20. **Single Sign-On:** Integration with enterprise identity?
   757вҶ’

# Maintenance Compliance Dashboard Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-006
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **Maintenance Compliance Dashboard**, enabling management to monitor PM schedule adherence, cost compliance against budget, and identify variance trends across the 128-train fleet over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
|------|---------------|------------|
| Maintenance Manager | Monitor PM compliance, identify gaps | Schedule Adherence |
| Finance Controller | Track budget vs actual costs | Cost Compliance |
| Contract Manager | Ensure SLA adherence | KPI Summary |
| Executive | High-level oversight | Executive Summary |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 5:** "Maintenance compliance status"
- **Section 5:** "Monthly/yearly cost compliance"
- **Section 6:** "Actual vs planned mileage" monitoring
- **Section 8:** Compliance reporting requirements

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Compliance Dashboard
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Executive Summary (Default)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ KPI Cards
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Compliance Score Gauge
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Monthly Trend
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Alert Summary
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Schedule Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ PM Compliance Rate
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Backlog Status
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Fleet Coverage
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Overdue Analysis
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Cost Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Budget vs Actual
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Variance Analysis
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Category Breakdown
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Forecast Projection
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Mileage Compliance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Planned vs Actual
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Fleet Balance
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Deviation Trends
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Train-Level Analysis
в””в”Җв”Җ Compliance Reports
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Monthly Report
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Annual Report
    в””в”Җв”Җ Custom Report Builder
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Executive | Schedule | Cost | Mileage | Reports
- **Breadcrumb:** Compliance > [Section] > [Period]
- **Period Selector:** Global control affecting all views

---

## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 8.1 Proximity

- **KPI Cards:** Grouped in a single row for quick scanning
- **Chart + Legend:** Legend positioned close to chart
- **Table + Actions:** Filter controls adjacent to table

### 8.2 Similarity

- **Status Colors:** Consistent across all views
  - рҹҹў Green = Under budget / On target / Compliant
  - рҹҹЎ Yellow = Warning / Approaching threshold
  - рҹ”ҙ Red = Over budget / Overdue / Critical
- **Card Design:** Same border radius, shadow, padding

### 8.3 Enclosure

- **Sections:** Clear card boundaries with subtle shadows
- **Tables:** Header row visually distinct from data rows
- **Charts:** Contained within labeled containers

### 8.4 Continuity

- **Trend Lines:** Smooth curves leading eye through time
- **Waterfall:** Connected bars showing flow from budget to actual
- **Progress Bars:** Horizontal continuity for completion status

### 8.5 Figure-Ground

- **Charts:** White background with colored data
- **Alerts:** Elevated cards on lighter background
- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention

### 8.6 Common Fate

- **Interactive Highlights:** Hovering chart point highlights related table row
- **Filter Application:** All components update simultaneously

---

## 9. Color System

### 9.1 Compliance Status Colors

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| Compliant / Under Budget | Green | #22C55E | Target met or exceeded |
| On Target | Blue | #3B82F6 | Within tolerance |
| Warning | Amber | #F59E0B | Approaching threshold |
| Non-Compliant / Over Budget | Red | #EF4444 | Exceeds tolerance |

### 9.2 Variance Colors

| Variance | Color | Meaning |
|----------|-------|---------|
| Favorable (savings) | Green #22C55E | Actual < Budget |
| Neutral | Gray #6B7280 | Within Вұ2% |
| Unfavorable (overrun) | Red #EF4444 | Actual > Budget |

### 9.3 Chart Palette

| Element | Color | Usage |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Actual | #3B82F6 (Blue) | Primary data series |
| Budget/Planned | #94A3B8 (Gray) | Comparison baseline |
| Target Line | #22C55E (Green) | Goal indicator |
| Kinki Sharyo | #8B5CF6 (Purple) | Fleet identifier |
| Alstom | #06B6D4 (Cyan) | Fleet identifier |

## 10. Responsive Design

### 10.1 Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout Changes |
|------------|-------|----------------|
| Desktop XL | 1920px+ | Full 4-column KPI row |
| Desktop | 1440px | 4-column KPI, charts side-by-side |
| Laptop | 1280px | 2x2 KPI grid, stacked charts |
| Tablet | 1024px | 2x2 KPI grid, single-column charts |
| Mobile | 640px | Single column, scrollable KPIs |

```

---

## 11. Interaction Patterns

### 11.1 Period Selection

- **Presets:** Month, Quarter, YTD, Custom
- **Custom Range:** Date picker with validation
- **Quick Navigation:** Previous/Next arrows
- **All Views Sync:** Period change updates all tabs

### 11.2 Drill-Down Navigation

| Click Target | Destination |
|--------------|-------------|
| PM Compliance KPI | Schedule Compliance tab |
| Cost KPI | Cost Compliance tab |
| Alert item | Relevant detail view |
| Chart data point | Detailed breakdown modal |
| Table row | Entity detail page |

### 11.3 Export Options

| Format | Content | Use Case |
|--------|---------|----------|
| PDF | Full report with charts | Executive presentations |
| Excel | Raw data with formulas | Further analysis |
| CSV | Table data only | System integration |
| Image | Individual charts | Reports/emails |

---

## 12. Performance Requirements

### 12.1 Load Times

| Metric | Target | Maximum |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
| Tab Switch | <500ms | 1s |
| Period Change | <1s | 2s |
| Export Generation | <5s | 15s |

### 12.2 Data Refresh

| Data Type | Refresh Interval |
|-----------|------------------|
| Real-time KPIs | 5 minutes |
| Charts/Trends | On demand |
| Reports | Daily batch |
| Alerts | Push notification |

---

## 13. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 13.1 Business Logic

1. **Compliance aggregation:** How to weight sub-metrics into overall score?
2. **Tolerance bands:** Different tolerances for different PM levels?
3. **Cost allocation:** How are shared costs split between fleets?
4. **Baseline adjustments:** When can budget baselines be updated?
5. **Seasonality:** Are there seasonal patterns in mileage/cost compliance?

### 13.2 UX Challenges

6. **Alert fatigue:** How many alerts before users ignore them?
7. **Drill-down depth:** How many levels before users get lost?
8. **Comparison periods:** What comparisons are most valuable (MoM, YoY)?
9. **Goal setting:** Who sets compliance targets and can they be adjusted?
10. **Notification preferences:** Email vs in-app vs SMS for alerts?

### 13.3 Technical Challenges

11. **Historical data retention:** How far back to keep detailed data?
12. **Data reconciliation:** Handling discrepancies between systems?
13. **Offline access:** Do users need offline compliance reports?
14. **Multi-currency:** Are there cross-border cost considerations?
15. **Audit trail:** What compliance changes need to be logged?

### 13.4 Integration

16. **Maximo cost sync:** How are actual costs imported?
17. **Budget import:** Source system for budget data?
18. **External reporting:** Format for regulatory compliance reports?
19. **Dashboard embedding:** Can dashboards be embedded elsewhere?
20. **Single Sign-On:** Integration with enterprise identity?

---

## 14. Sources & References

### 14.1 Maintenance KPIs

- [Fabrico: 7 Essential Maintenance KPIs for 2025](https://www.fabrico.io/blog/maintenance-kpi-dashboard-guide/)
- [MaintainX: Maintenance KPIs Guide](https://www.getmaintainx.com/blog/beginners-guide-maintenance-kpis)
- [Limble CMMS: The Simple Guide to Maintenance KPIs](https://limblecmms.com/learn/metrics/kpi/)
- [eMaint: Guide to Maintenance KPIs](https://www.emaint.com/works/cmms-kpi/)

### 14.2 Variance & Financial Visualization

- [ZebraBI: Variance Analysis in Excel](https://zebrabi.com/variance-analysis-excel/)
- [Microsoft Power BI: Actual vs Budget Charts](https://powerbi.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/actual-vs-budget-which-chart-is-effective/)
- [Vena Solutions: Variance Report Best Practices](https://www.venasolutions.com/blog/4-best-practices-creating-variance-analysis-report-excel)
- [Explo: Compliance Dashboards Best Practices](https://www.explo.co/blog/compliance-dashboards-compliance-management-reporting)

### 14.3 Fleet Maintenance

- [RTA Fleet: Increasing PM Compliance](https://rtafleet.com/blog/fleet-success-story-increasing-vehicle-availability-pm-compliance)
- [Fleetio: Fleet Maintenance Spreadsheet](https://www.fleetio.com/blog/fleet-maintenance-spreadsheet)
- [Fiix: Preventive Maintenance Compliance](https://fiixsoftware.com/maintenance-metrics/preventive-maintenance-compliance/)

---

## 15. Appendix: TypeScript Types Reference

See `src/mileage/types/resource.ts` for existing cost and resource types:
- `CostBaseline` - Per-activity cost baseline
- `CostProjection` - Period cost forecast
- `CostCompliance` - Budget vs actual tracking
- `ManHourAllocation` - Resource utilization
- `ManHourConfig` - Capacity configuration

---

*Document Version: 1.0 | Last Updated: 2026-01-08*

    12вҶ’### 1.1 Purpose
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’This specification defines the user experience for the **What-If Scenario / Simulation** module, enabling planners to model different mileage management strategies, compare outcomes, and make data-driven decisions about fleet utilization, maintenance scheduling, and resource allocation over the 12-year contract period.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’### 1.2 Target Users
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
    19вҶ’|------|---------------|------------|
    20вҶ’| Maintenance Planner | Model PM schedule variations | Schedule Scenarios |
    21вҶ’| Operations Manager | Test run assignment strategies | Utilization Scenarios |
    22вҶ’| Fleet Manager | Balance mileage distribution | Fleet Strategy |
    23вҶ’| Finance Controller | Evaluate cost impact of decisions | Cost Projections |
    24вҶ’| Executive | Compare strategic options | Summary Comparison |
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’- **Section 4.2:** "System shall have the ability to do what if scenarios"
    29вҶ’- **Section 5:** "Recommend and adjust maintenance plan based on simulation"
    30вҶ’- **Section 6:** "Mileage deviation tracking" and "10% underrun threshold"
    31вҶ’- **Section 8:** "Manpower planning with adjustment scenarios"
    32вҶ’

    40вҶ’| **SAP Analytics Cloud** | Driver-based forecasting | Parameter adjustment with rolling forecasts |
    41вҶ’| **AnyLogic** | Monte Carlo simulation | Probability distribution visualization |

   214вҶ’### 4.2 Parameter Slider Design
   215вҶ’
   216вҶ’Following Nielsen Norman Group's slider design guidelines:
   217вҶ’
   218вҶ’**Slider Design Requirements:**
   219вҶ’- **Thumb Size:** 32x32px with 44x44px touch target
   220вҶ’- **Track Width:** Full container width minus 32px padding
   221вҶ’- **Value Display:** Current value shown above slider thumb
   222вҶ’- **Step Indicators:** Tick marks at 25% intervals
   223вҶ’- **Real-Time Feedback:** Preview updates as slider moves
   224вҶ’- **Reset Button:** Small "вҶә" icon to restore default value
   225вҶ’
   226вҶ’```
   227вҶ’PM Window Buffer
   228вҶ’[вҶә]    5%
   229вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв—Ҹв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   230вҶ’0%   5%    10%   15%   20%   25%
   231вҶ’Early PM вҶҗв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ Late PM
   232вҶ’```
   233вҶ’
   234вҶ’**Sources:**
   235вҶ’- [NN/G Slider Design Rules of Thumb](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
   236вҶ’- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’### 4.3 Parameter Definitions
   239вҶ’
   240вҶ’| Parameter | Range | Default | Impact |
   241вҶ’|-----------|-------|---------|--------|
   242вҶ’| **PM Window Buffer** | 0-25% | 10% | How early before PM threshold to schedule |
   243вҶ’| **Mileage Tolerance** | -10% to +15% | 0% | Deviation from planned daily mileage |
   244вҶ’| **Fleet Balance Target** | 70-100% | 90% | How evenly distributed mileage should be |
   245вҶ’| **Man-Hours Available** | 50-100% | 90% | Resource capacity utilization |
   246вҶ’| **Workshop Tracks** | 1-6 | 4 | Concurrent PM capacity |
   247вҶ’| **Overtime Cap** | 0-30% | 15% | Maximum overtime percentage |
   248вҶ’
   249вҶ’### 4.4 Real-Time Preview Panel
   250вҶ’
   251вҶ’The preview panel updates within 500ms of parameter changes, showing:
   252вҶ’
   253вҶ’1. **Projected Outcomes:** KPI delta from baseline
   254вҶ’2. **Fleet Impact:** Progress bars by fleet type
   255вҶ’3. **Risk Indicators:** Warnings for constraint violations
   256вҶ’4. **Key Metrics:** 3-4 most relevant numbers
   257вҶ’

   425вҶ’в”Ӯ  PM Buffer          в—Җв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв”Ӯв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–¶  +/- $180k       в”Ӯ
   426вҶ’в”Ӯ  Mileage Tolerance  в—Җв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв”Ӯв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–¶            +/- $120k       в”Ӯ
   427вҶ’в”Ӯ  Man-Hour Cap          в—Җв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв”Ӯв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–¶                     +/- $75k        в”Ӯ
   428вҶ’в”Ӯ  Fleet Balance              в—Җв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв”Ӯв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–¶                      +/- $45k        в”Ӯ
   429вҶ’в”Ӯ  Overtime %                    в—Җв”Ӯв–¶                         +/- $15k        в”Ӯ
   430вҶ’в”Ӯ                                 в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
   431вҶ’в”Ӯ                           Base Case                                         в”Ӯ
   432вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                             в”Ӯ
   433вҶ’в”Ӯ  Interpretation: PM Buffer has the highest impact on cost outcomes          в”Ӯ
   434вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   435вҶ’```
   436вҶ’
   437вҶ’### 7.2 Monte Carlo Distribution (Advanced)
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’For advanced users, show probability distribution of outcomes:
   440вҶ’
   441вҶ’```
   442вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   443вҶ’в”Ӯ  COST PROJECTION DISTRIBUTION (1000 simulations)                            в”Ӯ
   444вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                             в”Ӯ
   445вҶ’в”Ӯ           в–„в–„в–„в–„в–„                                                             в”Ӯ
   446вҶ’в”Ӯ         в–„в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–„                                                            в”Ӯ
   447вҶ’в”Ӯ        в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ                                                         в”Ӯ
   448вҶ’в”Ӯ       в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ                                                        в”Ӯ
   449вҶ’в”Ӯ      в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–„                                                      в”Ӯ
   450вҶ’в”Ӯ    в–„в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–„                                                     в”Ӯ
   451вҶ’в”Ӯ  в–„в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–„                                                 в”Ӯ
   452вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                                  в”Ӯ
   453вҶ’в”Ӯ  $2.0M        $2.4M        $2.8M        $3.2M                              в”Ӯ
   454вҶ’в”Ӯ                  вҶ‘                                                          в”Ӯ
   455вҶ’в”Ӯ            Most Likely: $2.36M                                              в”Ӯ
   456вҶ’в”Ӯ            5th Percentile: $2.18M                                           в”Ӯ
   457вҶ’в”Ӯ            95th Percentile: $2.72M                                          в”Ӯ
   458вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   459вҶ’```
   460вҶ’
   461вҶ’**Sources:**
   462вҶ’- [Monte Carlo Simulation Dashboard Visualization](https://dev3lop.com/financial-risk-visualization-monte-carlo-simulation-dashboards/)
   463вҶ’- [AnyLogic Monte Carlo in Business](https://www.anylogic.com/blog/monte-carlo-simulation-in-business/)
   464вҶ’
   465вҶ’---
   466вҶ’
   467вҶ’## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied
   468вҶ’
   469вҶ’### 8.1 Proximity
   470вҶ’
   471вҶ’- **Parameter Groups:** Related sliders grouped in labeled sections
   472вҶ’- **Comparison Cards:** Side-by-side scenarios with clear separation
   473вҶ’- **Action Buttons:** Primary actions grouped in toolbar
   474вҶ’
   475вҶ’### 8.2 Similarity
   476вҶ’
   477вҶ’- **Consistent Slider Design:** All parameter sliders have identical styling
   478вҶ’- **Status Colors:** Same color system across all views
   479вҶ’- **Card Layout:** Uniform card structure for scenarios
   480вҶ’
   481вҶ’### 8.3 Enclosure
   482вҶ’
   483вҶ’- **Panel Boundaries:** Clear card borders for each section
   484вҶ’- **Comparison Frames:** Distinct frames for each scenario being compared
   485вҶ’- **Result Groups:** Bordered sections for different metric categories
   486вҶ’
   487вҶ’### 8.4 Continuity
   488вҶ’
   489вҶ’- **Timeline Flow:** Left-to-right progression in projections
   490вҶ’- **Parameter Order:** Logical top-to-bottom priority ordering
   491вҶ’- **Visual Alignment:** Grid-based layout for comparison tables
   492вҶ’
   493вҶ’### 8.5 Figure-Ground
   494вҶ’
   495вҶ’- **Active Scenario:** Elevated shadow for selected scenario
   496вҶ’- **Hover States:** Subtle elevation on interactive elements
   497вҶ’- **Comparison Highlight:** Background tint on "winner" column
   498вҶ’
   499вҶ’### 8.6 Common Fate
   500вҶ’
   501вҶ’- **Linked Sliders:** Connected parameters animate together
   502вҶ’- **Comparison Sync:** Scroll position synced across compared scenarios
   503вҶ’- **Selection Propagation:** Multi-select actions apply uniformly
   504вҶ’
   505вҶ’---
   506вҶ’
   507вҶ’## 9. Interaction Patterns
   508вҶ’
   509вҶ’### 9.1 Create New Scenario Flow
   510вҶ’
   511вҶ’```
   512вҶ’[Start] вҶ’ Select Template вҶ’ Adjust Parameters вҶ’ Preview Results вҶ’ [Save/Run]
   513вҶ’          (or Blank)       (real-time update)   (validate)
   514вҶ’```
   515вҶ’
   516вҶ’### 9.2 Compare Scenarios Flow
   517вҶ’
   518вҶ’```
   519вҶ’[Select 2-4 scenarios] вҶ’ Choose View Mode вҶ’ Analyze Differences вҶ’ [Export/Apply]
   520вҶ’                         (Side/Overlay/Table)
   521вҶ’```
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’### 9.3 Parameter Adjustment Interaction
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’1. **Drag slider thumb** вҶ’ Value updates in real-time
   526вҶ’2. **Click track** вҶ’ Thumb jumps to click position
   527вҶ’3. **Keyboard arrows** вҶ’ Increment/decrement by step
   528вҶ’4. **Type value** вҶ’ Direct input in value field
   529вҶ’5. **Reset button** вҶ’ Return to default value
   530вҶ’
   531вҶ’### 9.4 Real-Time Feedback Timing
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’| Action | Response Time | Feedback Type |
   534вҶ’|--------|---------------|---------------|
   535вҶ’| Slider drag | < 100ms | Value label update |
   536вҶ’| Slider release | < 500ms | Preview panel update |
   537вҶ’| Run simulation | < 3s | Progress indicator |
   538вҶ’| Load comparison | < 1s | Side-by-side render |
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’---
   541вҶ’
   542вҶ’## 10. Responsive Design
   543вҶ’
   544вҶ’### 10.1 Desktop (вүҘ1200px)
   545вҶ’
   546вҶ’- Full side-by-side comparison (2-4 scenarios)
   547вҶ’- Multi-column parameter layout
   548вҶ’- Expanded charts with full legends
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’### 10.2 Tablet (768-1199px)
   551вҶ’
   552вҶ’- Stacked comparison (scroll between scenarios)
   553вҶ’- Single-column parameter layout
   554вҶ’- Condensed chart legends
   555вҶ’
   556вҶ’### 10.3 Mobile (<768px)
   557вҶ’
   558вҶ’- **Limited Functionality:** View results only, no scenario building
   559вҶ’- Swipe between scenarios
   560вҶ’- Collapsed parameter summary
   561вҶ’- Simplified chart views
   562вҶ’
   563вҶ’**Rationale:** Scenario building requires precise parameter adjustment that is difficult on small screens. Mobile users can review and compare existing scenarios but should use desktop/tablet for creation.
   564вҶ’
   565вҶ’---
   566вҶ’
   567вҶ’## 11. Accessibility Requirements
   568вҶ’
   569вҶ’### 11.1 Keyboard Navigation
   570вҶ’
   571вҶ’| Key | Action |
   572вҶ’|-----|--------|
   573вҶ’| Tab | Move between interactive elements |
   574вҶ’| Arrow Left/Right | Adjust slider value |
   575вҶ’| Enter | Confirm selection / Run simulation |
   576вҶ’| Escape | Cancel / Close modal |
   577вҶ’| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand panel |
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’### 11.2 Screen Reader Support
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’- **ARIA Labels:** All sliders have descriptive labels
   582вҶ’- **Live Regions:** Result changes announced
   583вҶ’- **Value Announcements:** Slider values read on change
   584вҶ’
   585вҶ’### 11.3 Color Contrast
   586вҶ’
   587вҶ’- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
   588вҶ’- Status indicators have icon+text (not color alone)
   589вҶ’- Chart series differentiated by pattern + color
   590вҶ’
   591вҶ’---
   592вҶ’
   593вҶ’## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   594вҶ’
   595вҶ’Through research and analysis, the following "unknown unknowns" were identified:
   596вҶ’
   597вҶ’### 12.1 Simulation Complexity
   598вҶ’
   599вҶ’1. **Multi-year compounding:** Scenarios spanning 12 years have compounding effects that are hard to visualize
   600вҶ’2. **Interdependent parameters:** Changing one slider may require automatic adjustment of others
   601вҶ’3. **Stochastic vs deterministic:** Users may expect different randomness handling
   602вҶ’4. **Partial scenario runs:** What if simulation is stopped mid-way?
   603вҶ’
   604вҶ’### 12.2 User Collaboration
   605вҶ’
   606вҶ’5. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same scenario?
   607вҶ’6. **Version history:** How to track changes to scenario parameters over time?
   608вҶ’7. **Approval workflow:** Should applying a scenario to live planning require approval?
   609вҶ’8. **Template ownership:** Who can create/modify shared templates?
   610вҶ’
   611вҶ’### 12.3 Data Quality
   612вҶ’
   613вҶ’9. **Input validation:** How to handle impossible parameter combinations?
   614вҶ’10. **Data freshness:** How recent must mileage data be for accurate simulation?
   615вҶ’11. **External factors:** How to model unplanned events (breakdowns, weather)?
   616вҶ’12. **Seasonal patterns:** Should scenarios automatically adjust for known peaks?
   617вҶ’
   618вҶ’### 12.4 Performance
   619вҶ’
   620вҶ’13. **Simulation time:** Long-horizon scenarios may take minutes - how to handle UX?
   621вҶ’14. **Cache invalidation:** When should cached results be recalculated?
   622вҶ’15. **Partial results:** Can users see preliminary results while simulation runs?
   623вҶ’16. **Resource limits:** How many concurrent simulations per user?
   624вҶ’
   625вҶ’### 12.5 Reporting Integration
   626вҶ’
   627вҶ’17. **Audit trail:** Must scenario decisions be logged for regulatory compliance?
   628вҶ’18. **Report embedding:** Can scenario results be embedded in external reports?
   629вҶ’19. **Data export format:** What formats do downstream systems require?
   630вҶ’20. **Historical comparison:** How long to retain old scenario results?
   631вҶ’
   632вҶ’---
   633вҶ’
   634вҶ’## 13. TypeScript Types Reference
   635вҶ’
   636вҶ’See: `src/mileage/types/scenario-ui.ts`
   637вҶ’
   638вҶ’Key types to be implemented:
   639вҶ’- `ScenarioStatus`, `ScenarioTemplate`, `ScenarioConfig`
   640вҶ’- `ScenarioParameter`, `ParameterSlider`, `ParameterConstraint`
   641вҶ’- `SimulationResult`, `SimulationMetrics`, `SimulationWarning`
   642вҶ’- `ComparisonView`, `ComparisonRow`, `ComparisonDelta`
   643вҶ’- `SensitivityAnalysis`, `TornadoChartData`, `MonteCarloDistribution`
   644вҶ’- `ScenarioBuilderState`, `ComparisonViewState`, `ResultsExplorerState`
   645вҶ’
   646вҶ’---
   647вҶ’
   648вҶ’## 14. Sources and References
   649вҶ’
   650вҶ’### Research Sources
   651вҶ’
   652вҶ’- [DELMIA Rail Planning Software](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations)
   653вҶ’- [DELMIA Virtual Twin for Rail Operations](https://blog.3ds.com/brands/delmia/transforming-rail-operations-for-a-smarter-future/)
   654вҶ’- [Scenario Modeling Software Tools](https://www.farseer.com/blog/scenario-modeling-software/)
   655вҶ’- [Anaplan vs Oracle EPM Comparison](https://www.selecthub.com/epm-software/anaplan-vs-oracle-epm/)
   656вҶ’- [Monte Carlo Simulation Dashboards](https://dev3lop.com/financial-risk-visualization-monte-carlo-simulation-dashboards/)
   657вҶ’- [Scenario Comparisons - Agentnoon](https://docs.agentnoon.com/scenarios/scenario-comparisons)
   658вҶ’
   659вҶ’### UX Design References
   660вҶ’
   661вҶ’- [NN/G Slider Design Guidelines](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
   662вҶ’- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)
   663вҶ’- [Dashboard UI/UX for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)
   664вҶ’- [UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
   665вҶ’- [Visual Comparison Techniques](https://dev3lop.com/visual-comparison-techniques-for-before-after-analysis/)
   666вҶ’
   667вҶ’### Chart & Visualization References
   668вҶ’
   669вҶ’- [Metabase: Choosing the Right Visualization](https://www.metabase.com/blog/the-right-visualization)
   670вҶ’- [Types of Comparison Charts](https://chartexpo.com/blog/types-of-comparison-charts)
   671вҶ’- [Monte Carlo Charts in Practice](https://businessmap.io/blog/monte-carlo-chart-graph)
   672вҶ’
   673вҶ’---
   674вҶ’

   676вҶ’

# What-If Scenario / Simulation UI Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-007
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 4.2, 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **What-If Scenario / Simulation** module, enabling planners to model different mileage management strategies, compare outcomes, and make data-driven decisions about fleet utilization, maintenance scheduling, and resource allocation over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
|------|---------------|------------|
| Maintenance Planner | Model PM schedule variations | Schedule Scenarios |
| Operations Manager | Test run assignment strategies | Utilization Scenarios |
| Fleet Manager | Balance mileage distribution | Fleet Strategy |
| Finance Controller | Evaluate cost impact of decisions | Cost Projections |
| Executive | Compare strategic options | Summary Comparison |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 4.2:** "System shall have the ability to do what if scenarios"
- **Section 5:** "Recommend and adjust maintenance plan based on simulation"
- **Section 6:** "Mileage deviation tracking" and "10% underrun threshold"
- **Section 8:** "Manpower planning with adjustment scenarios"

### 1.4 Competitor Research Insights

| Competitor | Key Feature | Applicable Insight |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|
| **DELMIA Quintiq** | Virtual Twin simulation | Full rail network modeling, scenario KPI comparison |
| **Anaplan** | Multi-dimensional scenarios | Unlimited what-if models with instant comparison |
| **Oracle EPM** | Scenario modeling capabilities | Best/worst/base case comparisons |
| **SAP Analytics Cloud** | Driver-based forecasting | Parameter adjustment with rolling forecasts |
| **AnyLogic** | Monte Carlo simulation | Probability distribution visualization |

**Sources:**
- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations)
- [Scenario Modeling Software Tools](https://www.farseer.com/blog/scenario-modeling-software/)
- [Anaplan vs Oracle EPM Comparison](https://www.selecthub.com/epm-software/anaplan-vs-oracle-epm/)

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
What-If Scenarios
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scenario Manager (Default)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scenario List
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Quick Create
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scenario Status
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Recent Comparisons
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scenario Builder
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Parameter Configuration
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Strategy Selection
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Constraint Settings
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Simulation Preview
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scenario Comparison
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Side-by-Side View
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Overlay View
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ KPI Comparison Table
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Sensitivity Analysis
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Results Explorer
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Timeline Projections
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Resource Impact
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Cost Projections
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Risk Analysis
в””в”Җв”Җ Saved Scenarios
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ My Scenarios
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Team Scenarios
    в””в”Җв”Җ Published Templates
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Manager | Builder | Compare | Results | Saved
- **Breadcrumb:** Scenarios > [Scenario Name] > [View]
- **Context Actions:** Clone, Compare, Export, Share

---

## 3. Scenario Manager View

### 3.1 Layout Structure (Desktop 1920x1080)

```

| **Balanced Strategy** | Optimize all factors | Equal weight on metrics | PM buffer: 10%, Tolerance: 0% |
| **Cost Optimized** | Minimize cost within SLA | Defer PM to latest safe point | PM buffer: 3%, Tolerance: +8% |
| **Seasonal Peak** | Handle demand spikes | Higher utilization during peaks | Variable by month |
| **Fleet Rebalance** | Equalize mileage | Target uniform distribution | Balance target: 98% |

### 4.2 Parameter Slider Design

Following Nielsen Norman Group's slider design guidelines:

**Slider Design Requirements:**
- **Thumb Size:** 32x32px with 44x44px touch target
- **Track Width:** Full container width minus 32px padding
- **Value Display:** Current value shown above slider thumb
- **Step Indicators:** Tick marks at 25% intervals
- **Real-Time Feedback:** Preview updates as slider moves
- **Reset Button:** Small "вҶә" icon to restore default value

```
PM Window Buffer
[вҶә]    5%
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв—Ҹв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
0%   5%    10%   15%   20%   25%
Early PM вҶҗв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ Late PM
```

**Sources:**
- [NN/G Slider Design Rules of Thumb](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)

### 4.3 Parameter Definitions

| Parameter | Range | Default | Impact |
|-----------|-------|---------|--------|
| **PM Window Buffer** | 0-25% | 10% | How early before PM threshold to schedule |
| **Mileage Tolerance** | -10% to +15% | 0% | Deviation from planned daily mileage |
| **Fleet Balance Target** | 70-100% | 90% | How evenly distributed mileage should be |
| **Man-Hours Available** | 50-100% | 90% | Resource capacity utilization |
| **Workshop Tracks** | 1-6 | 4 | Concurrent PM capacity |
| **Overtime Cap** | 0-30% | 15% | Maximum overtime percentage |

### 4.4 Real-Time Preview Panel

The preview panel updates within 500ms of parameter changes, showing:

1. **Projected Outcomes:** KPI delta from baseline
2. **Fleet Impact:** Progress bars by fleet type
3. **Risk Indicators:** Warnings for constraint violations
4. **Key Metrics:** 3-4 most relevant numbers

### 5.3 Comparison Color Coding

| Indicator | Symbol | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| рҹҹў Green | Better than baseline | Favorable outcome |
| рҹҹЎ Yellow | Within tolerance | Acceptable trade-off |
| рҹ”ҙ Red | Worse than baseline | Unfavorable outcome |
| в¬Ҷ Arrow Up | Increase | Change direction |
| в¬Ү Arrow Down | Decrease | Change direction |

### 5.4 Multi-Scenario Comparison (Up to 4)

When comparing 3-4 scenarios, use a horizontal table with:
- **Highlighted Winner:** Green background on best value per row
- **Color Gradient:** Heat map coloring from best to worst
- **Rank Indicator:** #1, #2, #3, #4 in corner of cells

### 6.3 Risk Analysis Panel

| Risk Category | Indicator | Threshold | Scenario Value | Status |
|---------------|-----------|-----------|----------------|--------|
| Mileage Overrun | Trains > 10% over | < 5 trains | 8 trains | рҹ”ҙ High |
| PM Backlog | Events > 7 days late | < 3 events | 2 events | рҹҹў Low |
| Resource Gap | Man-hours shortfall | < 500 MH/mo | 280 MH/mo | рҹҹў Low |
| Cost Overrun | Variance from budget | < 5% | 3.2% | рҹҹЎ Medium |
| Fleet Imbalance | Deviation from target | < 10% | 13% | рҹ”ҙ High |

## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 8.1 Proximity

- **Parameter Groups:** Related sliders grouped in labeled sections
- **Comparison Cards:** Side-by-side scenarios with clear separation
- **Action Buttons:** Primary actions grouped in toolbar

### 8.2 Similarity

- **Consistent Slider Design:** All parameter sliders have identical styling
- **Status Colors:** Same color system across all views
- **Card Layout:** Uniform card structure for scenarios

### 8.3 Enclosure

- **Panel Boundaries:** Clear card borders for each section
- **Comparison Frames:** Distinct frames for each scenario being compared
- **Result Groups:** Bordered sections for different metric categories

### 8.4 Continuity

- **Timeline Flow:** Left-to-right progression in projections
- **Parameter Order:** Logical top-to-bottom priority ordering
- **Visual Alignment:** Grid-based layout for comparison tables

### 8.5 Figure-Ground

- **Active Scenario:** Elevated shadow for selected scenario
- **Hover States:** Subtle elevation on interactive elements
- **Comparison Highlight:** Background tint on "winner" column

### 8.6 Common Fate

- **Linked Sliders:** Connected parameters animate together
- **Comparison Sync:** Scroll position synced across compared scenarios
- **Selection Propagation:** Multi-select actions apply uniformly

---

## 9. Interaction Patterns

### 9.1 Create New Scenario Flow

```
[Start] вҶ’ Select Template вҶ’ Adjust Parameters вҶ’ Preview Results вҶ’ [Save/Run]
          (or Blank)       (real-time update)   (validate)
```

### 9.2 Compare Scenarios Flow

```
[Select 2-4 scenarios] вҶ’ Choose View Mode вҶ’ Analyze Differences вҶ’ [Export/Apply]
                         (Side/Overlay/Table)
```

### 9.3 Parameter Adjustment Interaction

1. **Drag slider thumb** вҶ’ Value updates in real-time
2. **Click track** вҶ’ Thumb jumps to click position
3. **Keyboard arrows** вҶ’ Increment/decrement by step
4. **Type value** вҶ’ Direct input in value field
5. **Reset button** вҶ’ Return to default value

### 9.4 Real-Time Feedback Timing

| Action | Response Time | Feedback Type |
|--------|---------------|---------------|
| Slider drag | < 100ms | Value label update |
| Slider release | < 500ms | Preview panel update |
| Run simulation | < 3s | Progress indicator |
| Load comparison | < 1s | Side-by-side render |

---

## 10. Responsive Design

### 10.1 Desktop (вүҘ1200px)

- Full side-by-side comparison (2-4 scenarios)
- Multi-column parameter layout
- Expanded charts with full legends

### 10.2 Tablet (768-1199px)

- Stacked comparison (scroll between scenarios)
- Single-column parameter layout
- Condensed chart legends

### 10.3 Mobile (<768px)

- **Limited Functionality:** View results only, no scenario building
- Swipe between scenarios
- Collapsed parameter summary
- Simplified chart views

**Rationale:** Scenario building requires precise parameter adjustment that is difficult on small screens. Mobile users can review and compare existing scenarios but should use desktop/tablet for creation.

---

## 11. Accessibility Requirements

### 11.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Move between interactive elements |
| Arrow Left/Right | Adjust slider value |
| Enter | Confirm selection / Run simulation |
| Escape | Cancel / Close modal |
| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand panel |

### 11.2 Screen Reader Support

- **ARIA Labels:** All sliders have descriptive labels
- **Live Regions:** Result changes announced
- **Value Announcements:** Slider values read on change

### 11.3 Color Contrast

- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
- Status indicators have icon+text (not color alone)
- Chart series differentiated by pattern + color

---

## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

Through research and analysis, the following "unknown unknowns" were identified:

### 12.1 Simulation Complexity

1. **Multi-year compounding:** Scenarios spanning 12 years have compounding effects that are hard to visualize
2. **Interdependent parameters:** Changing one slider may require automatic adjustment of others
3. **Stochastic vs deterministic:** Users may expect different randomness handling
4. **Partial scenario runs:** What if simulation is stopped mid-way?

### 12.2 User Collaboration

5. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same scenario?
6. **Version history:** How to track changes to scenario parameters over time?
7. **Approval workflow:** Should applying a scenario to live planning require approval?
8. **Template ownership:** Who can create/modify shared templates?

### 12.3 Data Quality

9. **Input validation:** How to handle impossible parameter combinations?
10. **Data freshness:** How recent must mileage data be for accurate simulation?
11. **External factors:** How to model unplanned events (breakdowns, weather)?
12. **Seasonal patterns:** Should scenarios automatically adjust for known peaks?

### 12.4 Performance

13. **Simulation time:** Long-horizon scenarios may take minutes - how to handle UX?
14. **Cache invalidation:** When should cached results be recalculated?
15. **Partial results:** Can users see preliminary results while simulation runs?
16. **Resource limits:** How many concurrent simulations per user?

### 12.5 Reporting Integration

17. **Audit trail:** Must scenario decisions be logged for regulatory compliance?
18. **Report embedding:** Can scenario results be embedded in external reports?
19. **Data export format:** What formats do downstream systems require?
20. **Historical comparison:** How long to retain old scenario results?

   404вҶ’### 7.2 Builder Components
   405вҶ’
   406вҶ’| Component | Purpose | Configuration |
   407вҶ’|-----------|---------|---------------|
   408вҶ’| **Header** | Report title, logo, date | Title text, logo toggle, date format |
   409вҶ’| **Text** | Static text, descriptions | Rich text editor |
   410вҶ’| **KPI Card** | Single metric display | Data field, format, comparison |
   411вҶ’| **Table** | Data rows and columns | Columns, sort, filter, grouping |
   412вҶ’| **Chart** | Visual data representation | Type, series, axes, legend |
   413вҶ’| **Summary** | Aggregated statistics | Metrics, calculations |
   414вҶ’| **Page Break** | PDF pagination | Before/after page break |
   415вҶ’| **Image** | Logos, diagrams | Upload, URL, sizing |
   416вҶ’
   417вҶ’### 7.3 Data Binding
   418вҶ’
   419вҶ’Components can bind to data using:
   420вҶ’- **Field References:** `{train.mileage}`, `{maintenance.compliance}`
   421вҶ’- **Expressions:** `{SUM(mileage.daily)}`, `{AVG(cost.labor)}`
   422вҶ’- **Conditions:** `{IF(compliance > 90, "вң“", "вң—")}`
   423вҶ’- **Formatting:** `{FORMAT(date, "MMMM YYYY")}`
   424вҶ’

   462вҶ’

   465вҶ’- **Custom Reports:** 30 days (configurable)
   466вҶ’- **Archive:** Manual archive for permanent storage

   470вҶ’## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied
   471вҶ’
   472вҶ’### 9.1 Proximity
   473вҶ’
   474вҶ’- **Report Cards:** Related info grouped (name, description, formats)
   475вҶ’- **Parameters:** Grouped by category (period, fleet, details)
   476вҶ’- **Schedule Fields:** Frequency options near time options
   477вҶ’
   478вҶ’### 9.2 Similarity
   479вҶ’
   480вҶ’- **Report Icons:** Consistent category-specific icons
   481вҶ’- **Status Indicators:** Same color/icon system across views
   482вҶ’- **Action Buttons:** Uniform button styling
   483вҶ’
   484вҶ’### 9.3 Enclosure
   485вҶ’
   486вҶ’- **Report Cards:** Clear boundaries for each report
   487вҶ’- **Modal Windows:** Defined generation/schedule containers
   488вҶ’- **Builder Canvas:** Distinct component areas
   489вҶ’
   490вҶ’### 9.4 Continuity
   491вҶ’
   492вҶ’- **Generation Flow:** Left-to-right parameter progression
   493вҶ’- **Schedule Timeline:** Chronological list ordering
   494вҶ’- **Builder Canvas:** Top-to-bottom report structure
   495вҶ’
   496вҶ’### 9.5 Figure-Ground
   497вҶ’
   498вҶ’- **Active Report:** Elevated shadow on selected
   499вҶ’- **Drag Components:** Lifted appearance during drag
   500вҶ’- **Modals:** Dimmed background for focus
   501вҶ’
   502вҶ’---
   503вҶ’
   504вҶ’## 10. Accessibility Requirements
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’### 10.1 Keyboard Navigation
   507вҶ’
   508вҶ’| Key | Action |
   509вҶ’|-----|--------|
   510вҶ’| Tab | Navigate between fields/buttons |
   511вҶ’| Enter | Confirm selection / Generate |
   512вҶ’| Escape | Close modal / Cancel |
   513вҶ’| Arrow Keys | Navigate list items |
   514вҶ’| Space | Toggle checkboxes |
   515вҶ’
   516вҶ’### 10.2 Screen Reader Support
   517вҶ’
   518вҶ’- **ARIA Labels:** All buttons and inputs labeled
   519вҶ’- **Live Regions:** Progress updates announced
   520вҶ’- **Table Headers:** Proper scope attributes
   521вҶ’- **Status Changes:** Role="status" for notifications
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’### 10.3 Color Contrast
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
   526вҶ’- Status indicators use icon + text (not color alone)
   527вҶ’- Focus states clearly visible
   528вҶ’
   529вҶ’---
   530вҶ’
   531вҶ’## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’### 11.1 Report Content
   534вҶ’
   535вҶ’1. **Multi-language reports:** International stakeholders may need localized reports
   536вҶ’2. **Data currency:** Report should show data freshness/last sync time
   537вҶ’3. **Drill-down links:** PDF links to web app for live data
   538вҶ’4. **Digital signatures:** Compliance reports may need e-signatures
   539вҶ’5. **Watermarks:** Draft vs Final report distinction
   540вҶ’
   541вҶ’### 11.2 Distribution
   542вҶ’
   543вҶ’6. **Email size limits:** Large PDF attachments may be blocked
   544вҶ’7. **External recipients:** Security for sharing outside organization
   545вҶ’8. **Notification preferences:** Some users may prefer Slack/Teams
   546вҶ’9. **Time zone handling:** Scheduled reports across time zones
   547вҶ’10. **Holiday calendars:** Skip scheduled reports on holidays?
   548вҶ’
   549вҶ’### 11.3 Technical
   550вҶ’
   551вҶ’11. **Report caching:** How long to cache large reports?
   552вҶ’12. **Concurrent generation:** Queue management for multiple users
   553вҶ’13. **Partial failures:** Handling incomplete data sources
   554вҶ’14. **Version compatibility:** Excel format compatibility
   555вҶ’15. **Mobile report viewing:** Responsive PDF viewing
   556вҶ’
   557вҶ’### 11.4 Compliance
   558вҶ’
   559вҶ’16. **Audit trail:** Who accessed which reports and when
   560вҶ’17. **Data masking:** PII handling in shared reports
   561вҶ’18. **Retention compliance:** Different retention per report type
   562вҶ’19. **Report amendments:** How to handle corrections to published reports
   563вҶ’20. **Chain of custody:** For regulatory submissions
   564вҶ’
   565вҶ’---

   567вҶ’## 12. Standard Report Templates
   568вҶ’
   569вҶ’### 12.1 PM Compliance Report
   570вҶ’
   571вҶ’**Sections:**
   572вҶ’1. Executive Summary (1 page)
   573вҶ’2. Overall Compliance Metrics
   574вҶ’3. Compliance by PM Level
   575вҶ’4. Compliance by Fleet
   576вҶ’5. Overdue Events Detail
   577вҶ’6. Trend Analysis (12 months)
   578вҶ’7. Recommendations
   579вҶ’
   580вҶ’### 12.2 Fleet Mileage Status Report
   581вҶ’
   582вҶ’**Sections:**
   583вҶ’1. Summary Statistics
   584вҶ’2. Mileage Distribution Chart
   585вҶ’3. Train-by-Train Status Table
   586вҶ’4. PM Window Status
   587вҶ’5. Deviation Alerts
   588вҶ’6. Balance Analysis
   589вҶ’
   590вҶ’### 12.3 Budget vs Actual Report
   591вҶ’
   592вҶ’**Sections:**
   593вҶ’1. Cost Summary
   594вҶ’2. Variance Analysis (Waterfall)
   595вҶ’3. Category Breakdown
   596вҶ’4. Fleet Comparison
   597вҶ’5. Trend Analysis
   598вҶ’6. Forecast to Year-End
   599вҶ’
   600вҶ’### 12.4 Executive Summary Report
   601вҶ’
   602вҶ’**Sections:**
   603вҶ’1. KPI Dashboard (1 page)
   604вҶ’2. Key Highlights
   605вҶ’3. Risks and Issues
   606вҶ’4. Action Items
   607вҶ’5. 12-Month Outlook
   608вҶ’

# Report Generation Templates Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-008
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **Report Generation** module, enabling users to generate, schedule, customize, and export reports for maintenance compliance, mileage tracking, cost analysis, and operational performance across the 128-train fleet over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | Report Focus |
|------|---------------|--------------|
| Maintenance Manager | PM compliance, backlog reports | Schedule adherence |
| Operations Manager | Daily/weekly summaries | Utilization |
| Finance Controller | Cost reports, variance analysis | Budget compliance |
| Contract Manager | SLA compliance reports | KPI tracking |
| Executive | Executive summaries | High-level metrics |
| Auditor | Compliance documentation | Regulatory reports |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 5:** "System generates maintenance reports"
- **Section 5:** "Monthly/yearly cost compliance reports"
- **Section 6:** "Mileage status reports for all trains"
- **Section 8:** "Man-hour projection reports"

### 1.4 Competitor Research Insights

| Competitor | Key Feature | Applicable Insight |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|
| **Simply Fleet** | Free Excel/PDF templates | Multi-format export, budget templates |
| **ClickUp** | Excel compatibility | Cross-platform export |
| **Microsoft Report Builder** | Drag-and-drop design | Visual report designer |
| **Telerik Reporting** | Scheduled automation | Pixel-perfect + scheduling |
| **SafetyCulture** | Compliance checklists | Transportation-specific templates |

**Sources:**
- [Simply Fleet Templates](https://www.simplyfleet.app/free-template-checklists-fleet)
- [Microsoft Report Builder](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/report-builder/report-design-view-report-builder)
- [FRA Compliance Requirements](https://railroads.dot.gov/guidance)

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Reports
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Standard Reports (Default)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Report Library
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Report Preview
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Generate Report
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Export Options
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Scheduled Reports
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Schedule List
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Create Schedule
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Schedule History
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Distribution Settings
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Custom Reports
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Report Builder
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Template Library
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ My Templates
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Share Templates
в””в”Җв”Җ Report History
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Generated Reports
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Archive
    в””в”Җв”Җ Search Reports
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Standard | Scheduled | Custom | History
- **Breadcrumb:** Reports > [Category] > [Report Name]
- **Context Actions:** Generate, Schedule, Export, Share

---

## 3. Standard Report Library

### 3.1 Report Categories

| Category | Reports | Frequency |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| **Maintenance** | PM Compliance, Backlog Status, Overdue Analysis, Maintenance Summary | Daily/Weekly/Monthly |
| **Mileage** | Fleet Mileage Status, Deviation Report, Balance Analysis, Trend Report | Daily/Weekly |
| **Cost** | Budget vs Actual, Variance Analysis, Cost Breakdown, Forecast Report | Monthly/Quarterly |
| **Operations** | Run Assignment Summary, Utilization Report, Fleet Availability | Daily/Weekly |
| **Compliance** | SLA Compliance, Regulatory Report, Audit Trail | Monthly/Quarterly |
| **Executive** | Executive Summary, KPI Dashboard, 12-Month Outlook | Monthly |

### 3.2 Report Library Layout (Desktop 1920x1080)

```

### 3.3 Report Card Design

Each report in the library displays:
- **Icon:** Category-specific icon
- **Name:** Clear, descriptive title
- **Description:** 1-2 line summary
- **Formats:** Available export formats (PDF, Excel, CSV)
- **Last Generated:** Timestamp of last generation
- **Actions:** Generate, Schedule, Preview, Info

| **JSON** | API integration | Structured data for systems |

### 5.3 Excel Export Features

- **Multiple Sheets:** Summary, Detail, Charts, Raw Data
- **Formulas:** Calculated fields preserved
- **Pivot Tables:** Pre-configured pivot for analysis
- **Conditional Formatting:** Status colors applied
- **Headers:** Frozen rows for navigation
- **Filters:** Auto-filters enabled on data columns

### 6.3 Schedule Frequency Options

| Frequency | Options | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| **Daily** | Time, exclude weekends | 8:00 AM weekdays |
| **Weekly** | Day of week, time | Monday 9:00 AM |
| **Monthly** | Day of month, time | 1st of month, 10:00 AM |
| **Quarterly** | Quarter start/end, offset | Q1 start + 5 days |
| **On-Demand** | Triggered by event | On PM completion |
| **Custom** | Cron expression | Complex schedules |

### 7.2 Builder Components

| Component | Purpose | Configuration |
|-----------|---------|---------------|
| **Header** | Report title, logo, date | Title text, logo toggle, date format |
| **Text** | Static text, descriptions | Rich text editor |
| **KPI Card** | Single metric display | Data field, format, comparison |
| **Table** | Data rows and columns | Columns, sort, filter, grouping |
| **Chart** | Visual data representation | Type, series, axes, legend |
| **Summary** | Aggregated statistics | Metrics, calculations |
| **Page Break** | PDF pagination | Before/after page break |
| **Image** | Logos, diagrams | Upload, URL, sizing |

### 7.3 Data Binding

Components can bind to data using:
- **Field References:** `{train.mileage}`, `{maintenance.compliance}`
- **Expressions:** `{SUM(mileage.daily)}`, `{AVG(cost.labor)}`
- **Conditions:** `{IF(compliance > 90, "вң“", "вң—")}`
- **Formatting:** `{FORMAT(date, "MMMM YYYY")}`

### 8.2 Report Status

| Status | Icon | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Ready | вң“ | Report generated successfully |
| Processing | в—җ | Report generation in progress |
| Warning | вҡ  | Generated with warnings |
| Error | вң— | Generation failed |
| Expired | в—Ү | Report past retention date |

### 8.3 Retention Policy

- **Standard Reports:** 90 days
- **Compliance Reports:** 7 years (regulatory requirement per FRA)
- **Custom Reports:** 30 days (configurable)
- **Archive:** Manual archive for permanent storage

---

## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 9.1 Proximity

- **Report Cards:** Related info grouped (name, description, formats)
- **Parameters:** Grouped by category (period, fleet, details)
- **Schedule Fields:** Frequency options near time options

### 9.2 Similarity

- **Report Icons:** Consistent category-specific icons
- **Status Indicators:** Same color/icon system across views
- **Action Buttons:** Uniform button styling

### 9.3 Enclosure

- **Report Cards:** Clear boundaries for each report
- **Modal Windows:** Defined generation/schedule containers
- **Builder Canvas:** Distinct component areas

### 9.4 Continuity

- **Generation Flow:** Left-to-right parameter progression
- **Schedule Timeline:** Chronological list ordering
- **Builder Canvas:** Top-to-bottom report structure

### 9.5 Figure-Ground

- **Active Report:** Elevated shadow on selected
- **Drag Components:** Lifted appearance during drag
- **Modals:** Dimmed background for focus

---

## 10. Accessibility Requirements

### 10.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Navigate between fields/buttons |
| Enter | Confirm selection / Generate |
| Escape | Close modal / Cancel |
| Arrow Keys | Navigate list items |
| Space | Toggle checkboxes |

### 10.2 Screen Reader Support

- **ARIA Labels:** All buttons and inputs labeled
- **Live Regions:** Progress updates announced
- **Table Headers:** Proper scope attributes
- **Status Changes:** Role="status" for notifications

### 10.3 Color Contrast

- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
- Status indicators use icon + text (not color alone)
- Focus states clearly visible

---

## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 11.1 Report Content

1. **Multi-language reports:** International stakeholders may need localized reports
2. **Data currency:** Report should show data freshness/last sync time
3. **Drill-down links:** PDF links to web app for live data
4. **Digital signatures:** Compliance reports may need e-signatures
5. **Watermarks:** Draft vs Final report distinction

### 11.2 Distribution

6. **Email size limits:** Large PDF attachments may be blocked
7. **External recipients:** Security for sharing outside organization
8. **Notification preferences:** Some users may prefer Slack/Teams
9. **Time zone handling:** Scheduled reports across time zones
10. **Holiday calendars:** Skip scheduled reports on holidays?

### 11.3 Technical

11. **Report caching:** How long to cache large reports?
12. **Concurrent generation:** Queue management for multiple users
13. **Partial failures:** Handling incomplete data sources
14. **Version compatibility:** Excel format compatibility
15. **Mobile report viewing:** Responsive PDF viewing

### 11.4 Compliance

16. **Audit trail:** Who accessed which reports and when
17. **Data masking:** PII handling in shared reports
18. **Retention compliance:** Different retention per report type
19. **Report amendments:** How to handle corrections to published reports
20. **Chain of custody:** For regulatory submissions

---

## 12. Standard Report Templates

### 12.1 PM Compliance Report

**Sections:**
1. Executive Summary (1 page)
2. Overall Compliance Metrics
3. Compliance by PM Level
4. Compliance by Fleet
5. Overdue Events Detail
6. Trend Analysis (12 months)
7. Recommendations

### 12.2 Fleet Mileage Status Report

**Sections:**
1. Summary Statistics
2. Mileage Distribution Chart
3. Train-by-Train Status Table
4. PM Window Status
5. Deviation Alerts
6. Balance Analysis

### 12.3 Budget vs Actual Report

**Sections:**
1. Cost Summary
2. Variance Analysis (Waterfall)
3. Category Breakdown
4. Fleet Comparison
5. Trend Analysis
6. Forecast to Year-End

### 12.4 Executive Summary Report

**Sections:**
1. KPI Dashboard (1 page)
2. Key Highlights
3. Risks and Issues
4. Action Items
5. 12-Month Outlook

    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’### Purpose
    14вҶ’Define mobile-responsive design patterns for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform, enabling field access for operations and maintenance personnel while maintaining full functionality across all device sizes.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’### Scope
    17вҶ’- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
    18вҶ’- RFQ Section 6: "The web-based software shall be compatible with mainstream web browsers and shall be accessible on desktops and mobile devices."
    19вҶ’- Support for Operations, Maintenance, and Management roles on mobile
    20вҶ’- Offline-first capability for field environments
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’### Design Principles
    23вҶ’1. **Mobile-First**: Design for smallest screens first, then enhance
    24вҶ’2. **Progressive Enhancement**: Basic functionality works everywhere
    25вҶ’3. **Touch-Friendly**: Minimum 48px touch targets
    26вҶ’4. **Offline-First**: Core features available without connectivity
    27вҶ’5. **Performance**: Fast load times on 3G networks
    28вҶ’6. **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance on all devices

   491вҶ’## 8. Field Worker Optimizations
   492вҶ’
   493вҶ’### 8.1 Environmental Considerations
   494вҶ’
   495вҶ’Based on railway maintenance UX research:
   496вҶ’
   497вҶ’| Challenge | Solution |
   498вҶ’|-----------|----------|
   499вҶ’| Poor lighting | High contrast mode, larger fonts |
   500вҶ’| Gloves | Larger touch targets (64px+) |
   501вҶ’| Dirty screens | Simple swipe gestures, avoid multi-touch |
   502вҶ’| Attention span | Progressive disclosure, highlight key info |
   503вҶ’| Eyeglasses | Avoid small text (<16px) |
   504вҶ’| Outdoor glare | Dark mode option, high contrast |
   505вҶ’

   872вҶ’## 14. Gestalt Principles (Mobile)
   873вҶ’
   874вҶ’### 14.1 Proximity
   875вҶ’- Group related actions in bottom sheets
   876вҶ’- Cluster form fields logically
   877вҶ’- Maintain consistent spacing
   878вҶ’
   879вҶ’### 14.2 Similarity
   880вҶ’- Consistent card styling across views
   881вҶ’- Uniform icon language
   882вҶ’- Same color coding for status
   883вҶ’
   884вҶ’### 14.3 Enclosure
   885вҶ’- Cards with clear boundaries
   886вҶ’- Bottom sheet modal frames
   887вҶ’- Section separators
   888вҶ’
   889вҶ’### 14.4 Continuity
   890вҶ’- Smooth scroll animations
   891вҶ’- Gesture continuity (swipe flow)
   892вҶ’- Consistent navigation direction
   893вҶ’
   894вҶ’### 14.5 Figure-Ground
   895вҶ’- Elevated bottom sheets (shadow)
   896вҶ’- Modal overlay dimming
   897вҶ’- Active tab highlighting
   898вҶ’
   899вҶ’### 14.6 Common Fate
   900вҶ’- List items scroll together
   901вҶ’- Synchronized pull-to-refresh
   902вҶ’- Batch selection animations
   903вҶ’
   904вҶ’---
   905вҶ’
   906вҶ’## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)
   907вҶ’
   908вҶ’### Identified During Design
   909вҶ’
   910вҶ’1. **Data usage concerns**: Field workers may have limited data plans
   911вҶ’2. **Battery consumption**: Background sync impact on battery
   912вҶ’3. **Device fragmentation**: Android device diversity
   913вҶ’4. **Network reliability**: Rural/underground coverage gaps
   914вҶ’5. **PWA limitations**: iOS restrictions on background processing
   915вҶ’6. **Biometric auth**: Device-specific implementation differences
   916вҶ’7. **Camera quality**: QR scanning in poor lighting
   917вҶ’8. **Screen size extremes**: Very large tablets, foldables
   918вҶ’9. **Rugged device support**: Industrial-grade tablets
   919вҶ’10. **Multi-window support**: Split-screen on tablets
   920вҶ’11. **Handedness**: Left-handed users and navigation
   921вҶ’12. **Accessibility zoom**: Text scaling impact on layout
   922вҶ’13. **Dark mode sync**: System vs app preference
   923вҶ’14. **Update mechanism**: PWA update prompts
   924вҶ’15. **Storage limits**: Browser cache limits
   925вҶ’16. **Cross-device sync**: Session continuity
   926вҶ’17. **Print from mobile**: Report printing support
   927вҶ’18. **External keyboard**: Tablet with keyboard use case
   928вҶ’19. **Landscape lock**: Specific views that need landscape
   929вҶ’20. **Deep linking**: Navigation from push notifications
   930вҶ’
   931вҶ’---
   932вҶ’
   933вҶ’## 16. Implementation Checklist
   934вҶ’
   935вҶ’### Phase 1: Foundation
   936вҶ’- [ ] Set up responsive breakpoints
   937вҶ’- [ ] Implement mobile navigation
   938вҶ’- [ ] Create base card components
   939вҶ’- [ ] Set up PWA manifest
   940вҶ’
   941вҶ’### Phase 2: Core Features
   942вҶ’- [ ] Mobile dashboard view
   943вҶ’- [ ] Train list (cards)
   944вҶ’- [ ] Train detail view
   945вҶ’- [ ] Basic offline support
   946вҶ’
   947вҶ’### Phase 3: Field Worker
   948вҶ’- [ ] Quick check-in flow
   949вҶ’- [ ] Mileage entry (offline)
   950вҶ’- [ ] Task list view
   951вҶ’- [ ] QR code scanning
   952вҶ’
   953вҶ’### Phase 4: Advanced
   954вҶ’- [ ] Full offline sync
   955вҶ’- [ ] Push notifications
   956вҶ’- [ ] Background sync
   957вҶ’- [ ] Role-based views
   958вҶ’
   959вҶ’### Phase 5: Polish
   960вҶ’- [ ] Performance optimization
   961вҶ’- [ ] Accessibility audit
   962вҶ’- [ ] Device testing
   963вҶ’- [ ] PWA install flow
   964вҶ’
   965вҶ’---
   966вҶ’
   967вҶ’## 17. Change Log
   968вҶ’

# Mobile Responsive Design UX Specification

## MILEAGE-UX-010 - Rolling Stock Mileage Management

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Complete

---

## 1. Overview

### Purpose
Define mobile-responsive design patterns for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform, enabling field access for operations and maintenance personnel while maintaining full functionality across all device sizes.

### Scope
- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
- RFQ Section 6: "The web-based software shall be compatible with mainstream web browsers and shall be accessible on desktops and mobile devices."
- Support for Operations, Maintenance, and Management roles on mobile
- Offline-first capability for field environments

### Design Principles
1. **Mobile-First**: Design for smallest screens first, then enhance
2. **Progressive Enhancement**: Basic functionality works everywhere
3. **Touch-Friendly**: Minimum 48px touch targets
4. **Offline-First**: Core features available without connectivity
5. **Performance**: Fast load times on 3G networks
6. **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance on all devices

### Sources Referenced
- [Hicron: Fleet Management Dashboard Design](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/)
- [Volpis: UX Design of Fleet Management Apps](https://volpis.com/blog/user-experience-design-of-fleet-management-apps/)
- [Standard Beagle: Fleet Tablet App Design](https://standardbeagle.com/case-studies/fleet-tablet-app-design/)
- [Medium: Railway Maintenance SaaS UX Design](https://medium.com/@kirillgorban/smart-maintenance-saas-solution-for-railroad-switches-ux-design-approach-cd2c9851af4c)
- [Google: PWA Offline-First Architecture](https://developers.google.com/codelabs/pwa-training/pwa03--going-offline)
- [MDN: Offline and Background Operation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Progressive_web_apps/Guides/Offline_and_background_operation)

---

## 2. Responsive Breakpoints

### 2.1 Breakpoint Definitions

| Breakpoint | Width | Target Devices | Layout |
|------------|-------|----------------|--------|
| xs (Mobile) | <576px | iPhone SE, small phones | Single column |
| sm (Mobile Large) | 576-767px | iPhone 14, large phones | Single column |
| md (Tablet Portrait) | 768-991px | iPad portrait | 2-column |
| lg (Tablet Landscape) | 992-1199px | iPad landscape, small laptops | 3-column |
| xl (Desktop) | 1200-1399px | Standard monitors | Full layout |
| xxl (Desktop Large) | >=1400px | Large monitors, 4K | Full layout |

### 2.2 CSS Variables

```css
:root {
  /* Breakpoints */
  --breakpoint-xs: 0;
  --breakpoint-sm: 576px;
  --breakpoint-md: 768px;
  --breakpoint-lg: 992px;
  --breakpoint-xl: 1200px;
  --breakpoint-xxl: 1400px;

  /* Touch targets */
  --touch-target-min: 48px;
  --touch-target-comfortable: 56px;

  /* Spacing */
  --spacing-xs: 4px;
  --spacing-sm: 8px;
  --spacing-md: 16px;
  --spacing-lg: 24px;
  --spacing-xl: 32px;

  /* Typography (mobile-first) */
  --font-size-base: 16px;
  --font-size-sm: 14px;
  --font-size-xs: 12px;
  --font-size-lg: 18px;
  --font-size-xl: 20px;
  --font-size-h1: 24px;
  --font-size-h2: 20px;
  --font-size-h3: 18px;
}
```

---

## 3. Navigation Patterns

### 3.1 Desktop Navigation (>=992px)

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ [Logo]   Dashboard  Trains  Schedule  Compliance  Reports    [рҹ””][рҹ‘Ө]в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

- Horizontal top navigation bar
- Dropdown menus for sub-sections
- Fixed position (sticky)
- User profile and notifications in top-right

### 3.2 Tablet Navigation (768px-991px)

```

в”Ӯ [вҳ°]  [Logo]                                              [рҹ””][рҹ‘Ө] в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Dashboard в”Ӯ Trains в”Ӯ Schedule в”Ӯ Compliance в”Ӯ Reports              в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

- Condensed horizontal tabs
- Hamburger menu for secondary items
- Icons with abbreviated labels

### 3.3 Mobile Navigation (<768px)

**Bottom Tab Bar (Primary Navigation)**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ  рҹҸ       рҹҡӮ      рҹ“…      рҹ“Ҡ      вҳ°   в”Ӯ

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

**Hamburger Slide-Out Menu (Secondary Navigation)**

```

### 4.2 Collapsible Sections

For complex data on small screens:

## 6. Touch Interactions

### 6.1 Touch Targets

| Element | Minimum Size | Comfortable Size |
|---------|--------------|------------------|
| Buttons | 48x48px | 56x56px |
| List items | 48px height | 56px height |
| Form inputs | 48px height | 56px height |
| Tab bar items | 48x48px | 64px wide |
| Checkboxes | 24x24px (44px touch) | 32x32px |
| Radio buttons | 24x24px (44px touch) | 32x32px |

### 6.2 Gestures

| Gesture | Action |
|---------|--------|
| Tap | Select, activate |
| Long press | Context menu, drag start |
| Swipe right | Mark as done, reveal actions |
| Swipe left | Delete, archive |
| Pull down | Refresh |
| Pinch | Zoom (charts, maps) |
| Two-finger scroll | Horizontal scroll in tables |

### 7.2 Caching Strategy

| Data Type | Cache Strategy | Staleness |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| App shell (HTML/CSS/JS) | Cache first | Immediate update |
| Train list | Stale-while-revalidate | 5 minutes |
| Mileage data | Stale-while-revalidate | 1 hour |
| PM schedules | Network first, cache fallback | Real-time |
| User preferences | Cache first | On change |
| Images/icons | Cache first | Weekly update |

### 7.3 Offline Indicators

### 7.4 Optimistic UI Updates

```
User Action: Record mileage for KS-045

1. Immediate UI update (optimistic)
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯ вң“ Mileage recorded: 46,234 km     в”Ӯ
   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

2. Background sync attempt
   - If online: Confirm with server
   - If offline: Queue for later sync

3. On sync failure: Show retry option
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯ вҡ  Failed to sync mileage         в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ [Retry]  [Discard]                в”Ӯ
   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
