## 8. Field Worker Optimizations

### 8.1 Environmental Considerations

Based on railway maintenance UX research:

| Challenge | Solution |
|-----------|----------|
| Poor lighting | High contrast mode, larger fonts |
| Gloves | Larger touch targets (64px+) |
| Dirty screens | Simple swipe gestures, avoid multi-touch |
| Attention span | Progressive disclosure, highlight key info |
| Eyeglasses | Avoid small text (<16px) |
| Outdoor glare | Dark mode option, high contrast |

### 8.2 High Contrast Mode

```css
@media (prefers-contrast: more) {
  :root {
    --bg-primary: #000000;
    --bg-secondary: #1a1a1a;
    --text-primary: #ffffff;
    --text-secondary: #e0e0e0;
    --accent-color: #ffcc00;
    --error-color: #ff4444;
    --success-color: #44ff44;
  }
}
```

## 11. Performance Optimization

### 11.1 Performance Budgets

| Metric | Target | Mobile 3G |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| First Contentful Paint | <1.5s | <3s |
| Largest Contentful Paint | <2.5s | <4s |
| Time to Interactive | <3.5s | <7s |
| Total Blocking Time | <200ms | <600ms |
| Cumulative Layout Shift | <0.1 | <0.1 |
| Bundle size (gzipped) | <200KB | <200KB |

### 11.2 Optimization Techniques

| Technique | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| Code splitting | Route-based lazy loading |
| Image optimization | WebP format, srcset |
| Critical CSS | Inline above-fold styles |
| Prefetching | Priority routes |
| Skeleton screens | Show immediately while loading |
| Virtual scrolling | For long lists (128 trains) |

## 12. Accessibility (Mobile)

### 12.1 Screen Reader Support

```html
<!-- Train card -->
<article
  role="article"
  aria-label="Train KS-045, Kinki Sharyo fleet, 95% PM window, Warning status"
>
  <h3 id="train-title-045">KS-045</h3>
  <p aria-describedby="train-title-045">Kinki Sharyo</p>

  <div role="meter" aria-valuenow="95" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100">
    PM Window: 95%
  </div>

  <button aria-label="View details for train KS-045">
    View Details
  </button>
</article>
```

### 12.2 Focus Management

- Trap focus in modals/bottom sheets
- Return focus to trigger element on close
- Visible focus indicators (4px outline)
- Skip-to-content link

### 12.3 Reduced Motion

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

## 13. Testing Checklist

### 13.1 Device Testing Matrix

| Device | OS | Browser | Priority |
|--------|----|---------|---------|
| iPhone 14 Pro | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
| iPhone SE | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
| iPad Pro 12.9" | iPadOS 17 | Safari | High |
| Samsung Galaxy S23 | Android 14 | Chrome | High |
| Pixel 7 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |
| iPad Air | iPadOS 17 | Safari | Medium |
| Samsung Tab S9 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |

### 13.2 Test Scenarios

| Scenario | Test Method |
|----------|-------------|
| Offline functionality | Airplane mode toggle |
| Slow network | Chrome DevTools throttling |
| Touch targets | Manual testing with fingers |
| Orientation change | Device rotation |
| Keyboard navigation | External keyboard |
| Screen reader | VoiceOver / TalkBack |
| PWA install | Add to home screen |
| Background sync | Network restoration |

---

## 14. Gestalt Principles (Mobile)

### 14.1 Proximity
- Group related actions in bottom sheets
- Cluster form fields logically
- Maintain consistent spacing

### 14.2 Similarity
- Consistent card styling across views
- Uniform icon language
- Same color coding for status

### 14.3 Enclosure
- Cards with clear boundaries
- Bottom sheet modal frames
- Section separators

### 14.4 Continuity
- Smooth scroll animations
- Gesture continuity (swipe flow)
- Consistent navigation direction

### 14.5 Figure-Ground
- Elevated bottom sheets (shadow)
- Modal overlay dimming
- Active tab highlighting

### 14.6 Common Fate
- List items scroll together
- Synchronized pull-to-refresh
- Batch selection animations

---

## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)

### Identified During Design

1. **Data usage concerns**: Field workers may have limited data plans
2. **Battery consumption**: Background sync impact on battery
3. **Device fragmentation**: Android device diversity
4. **Network reliability**: Rural/underground coverage gaps
5. **PWA limitations**: iOS restrictions on background processing
6. **Biometric auth**: Device-specific implementation differences
7. **Camera quality**: QR scanning in poor lighting
8. **Screen size extremes**: Very large tablets, foldables
9. **Rugged device support**: Industrial-grade tablets
10. **Multi-window support**: Split-screen on tablets
11. **Handedness**: Left-handed users and navigation
12. **Accessibility zoom**: Text scaling impact on layout
13. **Dark mode sync**: System vs app preference
14. **Update mechanism**: PWA update prompts
15. **Storage limits**: Browser cache limits
16. **Cross-device sync**: Session continuity
17. **Print from mobile**: Report printing support
18. **External keyboard**: Tablet with keyboard use case
19. **Landscape lock**: Specific views that need landscape
20. **Deep linking**: Navigation from push notifications

1вҶ’# Alert & Notification System UX Specification
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## MILEAGE-UX-009 - Rolling Stock Mileage Management
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**Version:** 1.0
     6вҶ’**Last Updated:** 2026-01-08
     7вҶ’**Status:** Complete
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’---
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’## 1. Overview
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’### Purpose
    14вҶ’Design a comprehensive alert and notification system for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform that keeps stakeholders informed about critical events while preventing notification fatigue.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’### Scope
    17вҶ’- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
    18вҶ’- 12-year contract period
    19вҶ’- Multiple user roles (Operations, Maintenance, Management, Executive)
    20вҶ’- RFQ Section 5 compliance: "Email notification for underrun and overrun mileage"
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’### Design Principles
    23вҶ’1. **Assist, Don't Impede**: Notifications help users complete tasks
    24вҶ’2. **Context Matters**: Provide actionable information
    25вҶ’3. **User Control**: Prevent fatigue with customization
    26вҶ’4. **Tiered Urgency**: Not all alerts warrant same attention
    27вҶ’5. **Clear Escalation**: Automatic escalation for unacknowledged critical alerts
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### Sources Referenced
    30вҶ’- [Smashing Magazine: Design Guidelines For Better Notifications UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/07/design-guidelines-better-notifications-ux/)
    31вҶ’- [Toptal: Comprehensive Guide to Notification Design](https://www.toptal.com/designers/ux/notification-design)
    32вҶ’- [MagicBell: Notification System Design](https://www.magicbell.com/blog/notification-system-design)
    33вҶ’- [Carbon Design System: Notification Pattern](https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/notification-pattern/)
    34вҶ’- [Fleetio: Fleet Management Notifications](https://www.fleetio.com/features/fleet-management-notifications)
    35вҶ’- [Pitstop: Alert Management Workflows](https://pitstopconnect.com/2024/03/05/5-steps-effective-alert-management-workflows-fleet-operations/)
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’---
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’## 2. Alert Categories & Priorities
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’### 2.1 Priority Levels
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’| Priority | Label | Color | Response Time | Auto-Escalate |
    44вҶ’|----------|-------|-------|---------------|---------------|
    45вҶ’| P1 | Critical | #DC2626 (Red) | Immediate | 15 min |
    46вҶ’| P2 | Warning | #F59E0B (Amber) | 4 hours | 24 hours |
    47вҶ’| P3 | Info | #3B82F6 (Blue) | 24 hours | Never |
    48вҶ’| P4 | Success | #22C55E (Green) | N/A | Never |
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### 2.2 Alert Categories
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’#### Maintenance Alerts
    53вҶ’| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    54вҶ’|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    55вҶ’| PM Overdue | P1 | Train past PM threshold | Maintenance, Ops |
    56вҶ’| PM Critical | P1 | Train >= 95% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
    57вҶ’| PM Approaching | P2 | Train >= 80% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
    58вҶ’| PM Scheduled | P3 | PM scheduled within 7 days | Maintenance, Ops |
    59вҶ’| PM Completed | P4 | PM successfully completed | Maintenance, Ops |
    60вҶ’| Inspection Failed | P1 | Vehicle inspection item failed | Maintenance |
    61вҶ’| Fault Code Triggered | P2 | OBD fault code detected | Maintenance |
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’#### Mileage Alerts (RFQ Section 5)
    64вҶ’| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    65вҶ’|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    66вҶ’| Mileage Overrun | P2 | Train >110% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
    67вҶ’| Mileage Underrun | P2 | Train <90% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
    68вҶ’| Fleet Imbalance | P2 | Fleet deviation >15% | Operations |
    69вҶ’| Mileage Forecast Alert | P3 | Projected PM date conflict | Maintenance |
    70вҶ’| Daily Mileage Missing | P2 | No mileage recorded for 24+ hours | Operations |
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’#### Assignment Alerts
    73вҶ’| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    74вҶ’|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    75вҶ’| No Suitable Train | P1 | Cannot assign any train to route | Operations |
    76вҶ’| Assignment Conflict | P2 | Double-booking detected | Operations |
    77вҶ’| Schedule Not Published | P2 | Schedule not published by cutoff | Operations |
    78вҶ’| Low Recommendation Score | P3 | Best score <50% | Operations |
    79вҶ’| Assignment Changed | P3 | External system modified assignment | Operations |
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’#### Resource Alerts
    82вҶ’| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    83вҶ’|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    84вҶ’| Man-Hour Shortage | P1 | Gap < -1000 man-hours | Management |
    85вҶ’| Track Overbooked | P1 | Track utilization >100% | Maintenance |
    86вҶ’| Resource Near Capacity | P2 | Utilization >90% | Maintenance |
    87вҶ’| Overtime Required | P3 | Gap requires overtime | Management |
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’#### Cost Alerts
    90вҶ’| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    91вҶ’|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    92вҶ’| Budget Exceeded | P1 | Category >110% of budget | Management, Finance |

   386вҶ’## 6. Escalation Workflow
   387вҶ’
   388вҶ’### 6.1 Escalation Tiers
   389вҶ’
   390вҶ’```
   391вҶ’TIER 1 (Immediate)
   392вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Assigned user/team
   393вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: In-app + Push + Email
   394вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 15 minutes
   395вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 2
   396вҶ’
   397вҶ’TIER 2 (Supervisor)
   398вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Team supervisor
   399вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: In-app + Push + Email + SMS
   400вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 30 minutes
   401вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 3
   402вҶ’
   403вҶ’TIER 3 (Management)
   404вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Department manager
   405вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: All channels
   406вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 1 hour
   407вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 4
   408вҶ’
   409вҶ’TIER 4 (Executive)
   410вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Operations director
   411вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: All channels + Phone call
   412вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ Tracked until resolved
   413вҶ’```
   414вҶ’
   415вҶ’### 6.2 Escalation Matrix
   416вҶ’
   417вҶ’| Alert Type | Tier 1 | Tier 2 | Tier 3 | Tier 4 |
   418вҶ’|------------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
   419вҶ’| PM Overdue | Maintenance Tech | Maintenance Sup | Maintenance Mgr | Ops Director |
   420вҶ’| Track Overbooked | Scheduler | Planning Sup | Planning Mgr | Ops Director |
   421вҶ’| No Suitable Train | Dispatcher | Ops Supervisor | Ops Manager | Ops Director |
   422вҶ’| Budget Exceeded | Accountant | Finance Sup | Finance Mgr | CFO |
   423вҶ’| Integration Failure | IT Support | IT Lead | IT Manager | CTO |
   424вҶ’
   425вҶ’### 6.3 Acknowledgment Actions
   426вҶ’
   427вҶ’| Action | Effect |
   428вҶ’|--------|--------|
   429вҶ’| Acknowledge | Stops escalation, marks as "In Progress" |
   430вҶ’| Defer | Reschedules alert for later, requires reason |
   431вҶ’| Resolve | Closes alert, updates status |
   432вҶ’| Escalate | Manually escalates to next tier |
   433вҶ’| Reassign | Assigns to different user/team |
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’---

   623вҶ’```
   624вҶ’
   625вҶ’---
   626вҶ’
   627вҶ’## 11. Gestalt Principles Applied
   628вҶ’
   629вҶ’### 11.1 Proximity
   630вҶ’- Group related actions together
   631вҶ’- Alert metadata clustered (time, category, priority)
   632вҶ’- Settings grouped by function
   633вҶ’
   634вҶ’### 11.2 Similarity
   635вҶ’- Consistent color coding across all notification types
   636вҶ’- Uniform button styles for actions
   637вҶ’- Same icon language throughout
   638вҶ’
   639вҶ’### 11.3 Enclosure
   640вҶ’- Clear card boundaries for each notification
   641вҶ’- Panel borders for notification center
   642вҶ’- Modal frames for critical alerts
   643вҶ’
   644вҶ’### 11.4 Continuity
   645вҶ’- Left-to-right reading flow for actions
   646вҶ’- Timeline ordering (newest first)
   647вҶ’- Escalation tier visual progression
   648вҶ’
   649вҶ’### 11.5 Figure-Ground
   650вҶ’- Elevated toast notifications (shadow)
   651вҶ’- Modal overlay for blocking alerts
   652вҶ’- Unread vs read visual differentiation
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’### 11.6 Common Fate
   655вҶ’- Batch selection animations
   656вҶ’- Grouped notification bundling
   657вҶ’- Synchronized dismiss animations
   658вҶ’
   659вҶ’---
   660вҶ’
   661вҶ’## 12. States & Transitions
   662вҶ’
   663вҶ’### 12.1 Notification States
   664вҶ’
   665вҶ’| State | Visual Treatment |
   666вҶ’|-------|------------------|
   667вҶ’| Unread | Bold text, colored indicator dot |
   668вҶ’| Read | Normal weight, no indicator |
   669вҶ’| Acknowledged | Checkmark icon, muted colors |
   670вҶ’| Snoozed | Clock icon, italic text |
   671вҶ’| Resolved | Strikethrough, light gray |
   672вҶ’| Escalated | Red border, escalation indicator |
   673вҶ’
   674вҶ’### 12.2 Animation Specifications
   675вҶ’
   676вҶ’| Animation | Duration | Easing |
   677вҶ’|-----------|----------|--------|
   678вҶ’| Toast enter | 300ms | ease-out |
   679вҶ’| Toast exit | 200ms | ease-in |
   680вҶ’| Badge pulse | 1000ms | ease-in-out (loop) |
   681вҶ’| Mark read | 150ms | ease-out |
   682вҶ’| Dismiss | 200ms | ease-in |
   683вҶ’| Expand/collapse | 200ms | ease-out |
   684вҶ’
   685вҶ’---
   686вҶ’
   687вҶ’## 13. Integration Points
   688вҶ’
   689вҶ’### 13.1 External Systems
   690вҶ’
   691вҶ’| System | Integration Type | Alert Types |
   692вҶ’|--------|------------------|-------------|
   693вҶ’| Maximo | Bi-directional | Work orders, asset status |
   694вҶ’| Email Server | Outbound | All email notifications |
   695вҶ’| SMS Gateway | Outbound | P1 critical alerts |
   696вҶ’| Slack/Teams | Outbound (webhook) | Configurable |
   697вҶ’| Mobile App | Push (FCM/APNs) | All push notifications |
   698вҶ’
   699вҶ’### 13.2 Webhook Payload
   700вҶ’
   701вҶ’```json
   702вҶ’{
   703вҶ’  "id": "notif-001",
   704вҶ’  "timestamp": "2026-01-08T12:34:56Z",
   705вҶ’  "priority": "P1",
   706вҶ’  "category": "MAINTENANCE",
   707вҶ’  "type": "PM_OVERDUE",
   708вҶ’  "title": "PM Overdue: KS-045",
   709вҶ’  "body": "Train exceeded PM2 threshold by 1,234 km",
   710вҶ’  "trainId": "KS-045",
   711вҶ’  "fleetType": "KINKI_SHARYO",
   712вҶ’  "data": {
   713вҶ’    "currentMileage": 46234,
   714вҶ’    "threshold": 45000,
   715вҶ’    "overdue": 1234
   716вҶ’  },
   717вҶ’  "actions": [
   718вҶ’    { "id": "schedule", "label": "Schedule PM", "url": "/schedule/pm?train=KS-045" },
   719вҶ’    { "id": "view", "label": "View Details", "url": "/trains/KS-045" }
   720вҶ’  ]
   721вҶ’}
   722вҶ’```
   723вҶ’
   724вҶ’---
   725вҶ’
   726вҶ’## 14. Analytics & Metrics
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’### 14.1 Key Metrics
   729вҶ’
   730вҶ’| Metric | Description | Target |
   731вҶ’|--------|-------------|--------|
   732вҶ’| Acknowledgment Rate | % alerts acknowledged | >90% |
   733вҶ’| Response Time | Time to first action | <15 min (P1) |
   734вҶ’| Escalation Rate | % alerts escalated | <10% |
   735вҶ’| Dismiss Rate | % alerts dismissed without action | <20% |
   736вҶ’| Channel Effectiveness | Opens per channel | Track trend |
   737вҶ’
   738вҶ’### 14.2 Alert Dashboard Metrics
   739вҶ’
   740вҶ’```
   741вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   742вҶ’в”Ӯ Alert Analytics - Last 30 Days                                  в”Ӯ
   743вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   744вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
   745вҶ’в”Ӯ Total Alerts: 1,234    Acknowledged: 1,111 (90%)               в”Ӯ
   746вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
   747вҶ’в”Ӯ By Priority:           By Category:                            в”Ӯ
   748вҶ’в”Ӯ P1: 23 (2%)           Maintenance: 456 (37%)                   в”Ӯ
   749вҶ’в”Ӯ P2: 345 (28%)         Mileage: 321 (26%)                       в”Ӯ
   750вҶ’в”Ӯ P3: 567 (46%)         Assignment: 234 (19%)                    в”Ӯ
   751вҶ’в”Ӯ P4: 299 (24%)         Other: 223 (18%)                         в”Ӯ
   752вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
   753вҶ’в”Ӯ Avg Response Time:     Escalation Rate:                        в”Ӯ
   754вҶ’в”Ӯ P1: 8 min вң“           5.2% вң“                                   в”Ӯ
   755вҶ’в”Ӯ P2: 2.3 hr вң“          3.1% вң“                                   в”Ӯ
   756вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
   757вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   758вҶ’```
   759вҶ’
   760вҶ’---
   761вҶ’
   762вҶ’## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)
   763вҶ’
   764вҶ’### Identified During Design
   765вҶ’
   766вҶ’1. **Alert Fatigue Threshold**: At what point do users start ignoring alerts?
   767вҶ’2. **Cross-Shift Handoff**: How to handle alerts during shift changes?
   768вҶ’3. **Timezone Handling**: Users in different timezones seeing same alerts
   769вҶ’4. **Alert Correlation**: Related alerts should be grouped (e.g., PM overdue + budget impact)
   770вҶ’5. **False Positive Rate**: Threshold tuning to reduce noise
   771вҶ’6. **Network Reliability**: Push notification delivery in low-connectivity areas
   772вҶ’7. **Audit Requirements**: How long to retain alert history?
   773вҶ’8. **GDPR Compliance**: User data in notifications
   774вҶ’9. **Multi-Language Support**: Alert content localization
   775вҶ’10. **Role Change Impact**: What happens to subscriptions when role changes?
   776вҶ’11. **Delegation During Absence**: Auto-forwarding when user is OOO
   777вҶ’12. **Alert Storm Prevention**: Cascade prevention when system failure affects many trains
   778вҶ’13. **Acknowledgment Verification**: Ensuring the right person acknowledged
   779вҶ’14. **Alert De-duplication**: Same condition triggering multiple alerts
   780вҶ’15. **Historical Alert Replay**: Ability to replay alerts for training
   781вҶ’16. **Alert Priority Override**: Can users elevate/demote priorities?
   782вҶ’17. **Integration Failure Alerts**: What if Maximo sync fails silently?
   783вҶ’18. **Mobile App Permissions**: Users denying notification permissions
   784вҶ’19. **Email Deliverability**: Alerts going to spam folders
   785вҶ’20. **Alert Content Security**: Sensitive data in notification bodies
   786вҶ’
   787вҶ’---
   788вҶ’
   789вҶ’## 16. Responsive Design
   790вҶ’
   791вҶ’### 16.1 Desktop (>=1200px)
   792вҶ’- Full notification center panel (400px width)
   793вҶ’- Inline notifications span content width
   794вҶ’- Modal notifications centered (max 500px width)
   795вҶ’
   796вҶ’### 16.2 Tablet (768px - 1199px)
   797вҶ’- Notification panel as slide-over
   798вҶ’- Condensed notification cards
   799вҶ’- Touch-friendly action buttons
   800вҶ’
   801вҶ’### 16.3 Mobile (<768px)
   802вҶ’- Full-screen notification center
   803вҶ’- Swipe gestures for quick actions
   804вҶ’- Bottom sheet for actions menu
   805вҶ’- Large touch targets (48px minimum)
   806вҶ’
   807вҶ’---
   808вҶ’
   809вҶ’## 17. Implementation Notes
   810вҶ’
   811вҶ’### 17.1 Technology Stack Recommendations
   812вҶ’
   813вҶ’| Component | Recommended Tech |
   814вҶ’|-----------|------------------|
   815вҶ’| Real-time delivery | WebSocket / Server-Sent Events |
   816вҶ’| Push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) |
   817вҶ’| Email delivery | SendGrid / AWS SES |
   818вҶ’| SMS delivery | Twilio |
   819вҶ’| Message queue | RabbitMQ / AWS SQS |
   820вҶ’| State management | Redux / Zustand |
   821вҶ’| Animations | Framer Motion |
   822вҶ’
   823вҶ’### 17.2 Performance Targets
   824вҶ’
   825вҶ’| Metric | Target |
   826вҶ’|--------|--------|
   827вҶ’| Notification render | <100ms |
   828вҶ’| WebSocket connection | <500ms |
   829вҶ’| Push delivery | <2 seconds |
   830вҶ’| Email delivery | <30 seconds |
   831вҶ’| SMS delivery | <10 seconds |
   832вҶ’| Panel load (100 items) | <200ms |
   833вҶ’
   834вҶ’---
   835вҶ’
   836вҶ’## 18. Change Log
   837вҶ’
   838вҶ’| Version | Date | Changes |
   839вҶ’|---------|------|---------|
   840вҶ’| 1.0 | 2026-01-08 | Initial specification |
   841вҶ’
   842вҶ’---
   843вҶ’
   844вҶ’*Document prepared for MILEAGE-UX-009*
   845вҶ’*Rolling Stock Mileage Management System*
   846вҶ’

# Alert & Notification System UX Specification

## MILEAGE-UX-009 - Rolling Stock Mileage Management

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Complete

---

## 1. Overview

### Purpose
Design a comprehensive alert and notification system for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform that keeps stakeholders informed about critical events while preventing notification fatigue.

### Scope
- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
- 12-year contract period
- Multiple user roles (Operations, Maintenance, Management, Executive)
- RFQ Section 5 compliance: "Email notification for underrun and overrun mileage"

### Design Principles
1. **Assist, Don't Impede**: Notifications help users complete tasks
2. **Context Matters**: Provide actionable information
3. **User Control**: Prevent fatigue with customization
4. **Tiered Urgency**: Not all alerts warrant same attention
5. **Clear Escalation**: Automatic escalation for unacknowledged critical alerts

### Sources Referenced
- [Smashing Magazine: Design Guidelines For Better Notifications UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/07/design-guidelines-better-notifications-ux/)
- [Toptal: Comprehensive Guide to Notification Design](https://www.toptal.com/designers/ux/notification-design)
- [MagicBell: Notification System Design](https://www.magicbell.com/blog/notification-system-design)
- [Carbon Design System: Notification Pattern](https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/notification-pattern/)
- [Fleetio: Fleet Management Notifications](https://www.fleetio.com/features/fleet-management-notifications)
- [Pitstop: Alert Management Workflows](https://pitstopconnect.com/2024/03/05/5-steps-effective-alert-management-workflows-fleet-operations/)

---

## 2. Alert Categories & Priorities

### 2.1 Priority Levels

| Priority | Label | Color | Response Time | Auto-Escalate |
|----------|-------|-------|---------------|---------------|
| P1 | Critical | #DC2626 (Red) | Immediate | 15 min |
| P2 | Warning | #F59E0B (Amber) | 4 hours | 24 hours |
| P3 | Info | #3B82F6 (Blue) | 24 hours | Never |
| P4 | Success | #22C55E (Green) | N/A | Never |

### 2.2 Alert Categories

#### Maintenance Alerts
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| PM Overdue | P1 | Train past PM threshold | Maintenance, Ops |
| PM Critical | P1 | Train >= 95% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
| PM Approaching | P2 | Train >= 80% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
| PM Scheduled | P3 | PM scheduled within 7 days | Maintenance, Ops |
| PM Completed | P4 | PM successfully completed | Maintenance, Ops |
| Inspection Failed | P1 | Vehicle inspection item failed | Maintenance |
| Fault Code Triggered | P2 | OBD fault code detected | Maintenance |

#### Mileage Alerts (RFQ Section 5)
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| Mileage Overrun | P2 | Train >110% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
| Mileage Underrun | P2 | Train <90% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
| Fleet Imbalance | P2 | Fleet deviation >15% | Operations |
| Mileage Forecast Alert | P3 | Projected PM date conflict | Maintenance |
| Daily Mileage Missing | P2 | No mileage recorded for 24+ hours | Operations |

#### Assignment Alerts
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| No Suitable Train | P1 | Cannot assign any train to route | Operations |
| Assignment Conflict | P2 | Double-booking detected | Operations |
| Schedule Not Published | P2 | Schedule not published by cutoff | Operations |
| Low Recommendation Score | P3 | Best score <50% | Operations |
| Assignment Changed | P3 | External system modified assignment | Operations |

#### Resource Alerts
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| Man-Hour Shortage | P1 | Gap < -1000 man-hours | Management |
| Track Overbooked | P1 | Track utilization >100% | Maintenance |
| Resource Near Capacity | P2 | Utilization >90% | Maintenance |
| Overtime Required | P3 | Gap requires overtime | Management |

#### Cost Alerts
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| Budget Exceeded | P1 | Category >110% of budget | Management, Finance |
| Budget Warning | P2 | Category >95% of budget | Management |
| Cost Variance | P3 | Monthly variance >5% | Management |

#### System Alerts
| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
| Integration Failure | P1 | Maximo sync failed | IT, Admin |
| Data Sync Delayed | P2 | Sync >30 minutes late | IT |
| Report Generation Failed | P2 | Scheduled report failed | Admin |
| Session Expiring | P3 | User session ending soon | All |

---

## 3. Notification Types

### 3.1 Toast Notifications (Transient)

**Use For:** Real-time feedback, confirmations, non-critical updates

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ вң“  Assignment saved successfully                  Г— в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    Train KS-001 assigned to Route R-BLUE-01         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                          [Undo]     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Specifications:**
- Position: Top-right corner
- Duration: 5 seconds (auto-dismiss)
- Max visible: 3 stacked
- Animation: Slide in from right
- Action: Single CTA (e.g., Undo, View)

### 3.2 Inline Notifications (Contextual)

**Use For:** Page-specific alerts, form validation, workflow status

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ вҡ  Warning                                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 3 trains are approaching PM threshold this week.    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Review the PM schedule to ensure timely service.    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                              [View Schedule]        в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Specifications:**
- Position: Top of relevant content area
- Dismissible: Yes (with Г— button)
- Persistence: Until dismissed or condition resolved
- Actions: Up to 2 CTAs

### 3.3 Actionable Notifications (Require Response)

**Use For:** Critical alerts requiring user decision

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ вӣ” Critical: PM Overdue                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Train KS-045 has exceeded PM2 threshold by 1,234    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ km. Immediate action required.                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Current Mileage: 46,234 km                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ PM2 Threshold: 45,000 km                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Overdue By: 1,234 km (2.7%)                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ [Schedule PM Now]  [Defer with Approval]  [Dismiss] в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Specifications:**
- Position: Top of page or modal
- Dismissible: Yes (but logged)
- Persistence: Until action taken
- Actions: Up to 3 CTAs
- Audit: All dismissals logged

### 3.4 Modal Notifications (Blocking)

**Use For:** Critical decisions, confirmations with consequences

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                                                   Г— в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         вҡ  Confirm Schedule Override                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
```

---

## 6. Escalation Workflow

### 6.1 Escalation Tiers

```
TIER 1 (Immediate)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Assigned user/team
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: In-app + Push + Email
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 15 minutes
в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 2

TIER 2 (Supervisor)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Team supervisor
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: In-app + Push + Email + SMS
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 30 minutes
в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 3

TIER 3 (Management)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Department manager
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: All channels
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Wait time: 1 hour
в””в”Җв”Җ If not acknowledged вҶ’ TIER 4

TIER 4 (Executive)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notification to: Operations director
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Channels: All channels + Phone call
в””в”Җв”Җ Tracked until resolved
```

### 6.2 Escalation Matrix

| Alert Type | Tier 1 | Tier 2 | Tier 3 | Tier 4 |
|------------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| PM Overdue | Maintenance Tech | Maintenance Sup | Maintenance Mgr | Ops Director |
| Track Overbooked | Scheduler | Planning Sup | Planning Mgr | Ops Director |
| No Suitable Train | Dispatcher | Ops Supervisor | Ops Manager | Ops Director |
| Budget Exceeded | Accountant | Finance Sup | Finance Mgr | CFO |
| Integration Failure | IT Support | IT Lead | IT Manager | CTO |

### 6.3 Acknowledgment Actions

| Action | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| Acknowledge | Stops escalation, marks as "In Progress" |
| Defer | Reschedules alert for later, requires reason |
| Resolve | Closes alert, updates status |
| Escalate | Manually escalates to next tier |
| Reassign | Assigns to different user/team |

---

## 8. Alert Actions & Workflows

### 8.1 Quick Actions

| Action | Icon | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Schedule PM | рҹ“… | Opens PM scheduling modal |
| View Train | рҹҡӮ | Navigates to train details |
| View Analysis | рҹ“Ҡ | Opens relevant analysis view |
| Acknowledge | вң“ | Marks alert as acknowledged |
| Snooze | рҹ’Ө | Delays reminder (15m/1h/4h/Tomorrow) |
| Dismiss | Г— | Dismisses (logs action) |
| Share | рҹ“Ө | Shares via email/Slack |
| Comment | рҹ’¬ | Adds comment for audit |

### 8.2 Snooze Options

```

```

### 8.3 Bulk Actions

| Action | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| Mark All Read | Marks all visible as read |
| Acknowledge Selected | Batch acknowledge |
| Snooze Selected | Batch snooze with same duration |
| Export Selected | Export to CSV/PDF |
| Clear Resolved | Remove resolved notifications |

---

## 9. Notification Sounds & Haptics

### 9.1 Sound Configuration

| Priority | Default Sound | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| P1 Critical | Urgent alarm (3 beeps) | Cannot be muted |
| P2 Warning | Alert tone (2 beeps) | Can be muted |
| P3 Info | Subtle chime (1 beep) | Can be muted |
| P4 Success | Soft ping | Can be muted |

### 9.2 Mobile Haptics

| Priority | Haptic Pattern |
|----------|----------------|
| P1 Critical | Strong vibration (3x) |
| P2 Warning | Medium vibration (2x) |
| P3 Info | Light vibration (1x) |
| P4 Success | Subtle tap |

---

## 10. Accessibility

### 10.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

- **Color**: Not sole indicator; icons + text labels
- **Contrast**: 4.5:1 minimum for text
- **Focus**: Visible focus indicators
- **Screen Readers**: ARIA live regions for dynamic notifications
- **Keyboard**: Full keyboard navigation

### 10.2 ARIA Implementation

```html
<!-- Toast Notification -->
<div role="alert" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true">
  <span class="sr-only">Success notification:</span>
  Assignment saved successfully
</div>

<!-- Critical Alert -->
<div role="alertdialog" aria-live="assertive" aria-modal="true">
  <span class="sr-only">Critical alert:</span>
  PM Overdue: KS-045 requires immediate attention
</div>

<!-- Notification Badge -->
<button aria-label="Notifications, 7 unread items">
  <span class="badge">7</span>
</button>
```

---

## 11. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 11.1 Proximity
- Group related actions together
- Alert metadata clustered (time, category, priority)
- Settings grouped by function

### 11.2 Similarity
- Consistent color coding across all notification types
- Uniform button styles for actions
- Same icon language throughout

### 11.3 Enclosure
- Clear card boundaries for each notification
- Panel borders for notification center
- Modal frames for critical alerts

### 11.4 Continuity
- Left-to-right reading flow for actions
- Timeline ordering (newest first)
- Escalation tier visual progression

### 11.5 Figure-Ground
- Elevated toast notifications (shadow)
- Modal overlay for blocking alerts
- Unread vs read visual differentiation

### 11.6 Common Fate
- Batch selection animations
- Grouped notification bundling
- Synchronized dismiss animations

---

## 12. States & Transitions

### 12.1 Notification States

| State | Visual Treatment |
|-------|------------------|
| Unread | Bold text, colored indicator dot |
| Read | Normal weight, no indicator |
| Acknowledged | Checkmark icon, muted colors |
| Snoozed | Clock icon, italic text |
| Resolved | Strikethrough, light gray |
| Escalated | Red border, escalation indicator |

### 12.2 Animation Specifications

| Animation | Duration | Easing |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| Toast enter | 300ms | ease-out |
| Toast exit | 200ms | ease-in |
| Badge pulse | 1000ms | ease-in-out (loop) |
| Mark read | 150ms | ease-out |
| Dismiss | 200ms | ease-in |
| Expand/collapse | 200ms | ease-out |

---

## 13. Integration Points

### 13.1 External Systems

| System | Integration Type | Alert Types |
|--------|------------------|-------------|
| Maximo | Bi-directional | Work orders, asset status |
| Email Server | Outbound | All email notifications |
| SMS Gateway | Outbound | P1 critical alerts |
| Slack/Teams | Outbound (webhook) | Configurable |
| Mobile App | Push (FCM/APNs) | All push notifications |

### 13.2 Webhook Payload

```json
{
  "id": "notif-001",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-08T12:34:56Z",
  "priority": "P1",
  "category": "MAINTENANCE",
  "type": "PM_OVERDUE",
  "title": "PM Overdue: KS-045",
  "body": "Train exceeded PM2 threshold by 1,234 km",
  "trainId": "KS-045",
  "fleetType": "KINKI_SHARYO",
  "data": {
    "currentMileage": 46234,
    "threshold": 45000,
    "overdue": 1234
  },
  "actions": [
    { "id": "schedule", "label": "Schedule PM", "url": "/schedule/pm?train=KS-045" },
    { "id": "view", "label": "View Details", "url": "/trains/KS-045" }
  ]
}
```

---

## 14. Analytics & Metrics

### 14.1 Key Metrics

| Metric | Description | Target |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Acknowledgment Rate | % alerts acknowledged | >90% |
| Response Time | Time to first action | <15 min (P1) |
| Escalation Rate | % alerts escalated | <10% |
| Dismiss Rate | % alerts dismissed without action | <20% |
| Channel Effectiveness | Opens per channel | Track trend |

### 14.2 Alert Dashboard Metrics

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

```

---

## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)

### Identified During Design

1. **Alert Fatigue Threshold**: At what point do users start ignoring alerts?
2. **Cross-Shift Handoff**: How to handle alerts during shift changes?
3. **Timezone Handling**: Users in different timezones seeing same alerts
4. **Alert Correlation**: Related alerts should be grouped (e.g., PM overdue + budget impact)
5. **False Positive Rate**: Threshold tuning to reduce noise
6. **Network Reliability**: Push notification delivery in low-connectivity areas
7. **Audit Requirements**: How long to retain alert history?
8. **GDPR Compliance**: User data in notifications
9. **Multi-Language Support**: Alert content localization
10. **Role Change Impact**: What happens to subscriptions when role changes?
11. **Delegation During Absence**: Auto-forwarding when user is OOO
12. **Alert Storm Prevention**: Cascade prevention when system failure affects many trains
13. **Acknowledgment Verification**: Ensuring the right person acknowledged
14. **Alert De-duplication**: Same condition triggering multiple alerts
15. **Historical Alert Replay**: Ability to replay alerts for training
16. **Alert Priority Override**: Can users elevate/demote priorities?
17. **Integration Failure Alerts**: What if Maximo sync fails silently?
18. **Mobile App Permissions**: Users denying notification permissions
19. **Email Deliverability**: Alerts going to spam folders
20. **Alert Content Security**: Sensitive data in notification bodies

---

## 16. Responsive Design

### 16.1 Desktop (>=1200px)
- Full notification center panel (400px width)
- Inline notifications span content width
- Modal notifications centered (max 500px width)

### 16.2 Tablet (768px - 1199px)
- Notification panel as slide-over
- Condensed notification cards
- Touch-friendly action buttons

### 16.3 Mobile (<768px)
- Full-screen notification center
- Swipe gestures for quick actions
- Bottom sheet for actions menu
- Large touch targets (48px minimum)

---

## 17. Implementation Notes

### 17.1 Technology Stack Recommendations

| Component | Recommended Tech |
|-----------|------------------|
| Real-time delivery | WebSocket / Server-Sent Events |
| Push notifications | Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) |
| Email delivery | SendGrid / AWS SES |
| SMS delivery | Twilio |
| Message queue | RabbitMQ / AWS SQS |
| State management | Redux / Zustand |
| Animations | Framer Motion |

### 17.2 Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Notification render | <100ms |
| WebSocket connection | <500ms |
| Push delivery | <2 seconds |
| Email delivery | <30 seconds |
| SMS delivery | <10 seconds |
| Panel load (100 items) | <200ms |

---

## 18. Change Log

| Version | Date | Changes |
|---------|------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-01-08 | Initial specification |

---

/**
 * Mileage Management Types - Public API
 *
 * Re-exports all types for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System.
 * Supports 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) over 12-year contract.
 */

// Train and Fleet types
export type {
  FleetType,
  FitnessStatus,
  TrainStatus,
  PMLevel,
  Train,
  NextPMDue,
  MaintenanceHistoryEntry,
  FleetSummary,
  FleetComposition,
  FleetConfiguration,
} from './train';

export {
  DEFAULT_PM_INTERVALS,
  DEFAULT_FLEET_CONFIGS,
  PM_LEVELS_ORDER,
  generateTrainId,
  getNextPMLevel,
  calculatePMDueDate,
} from './train';

// Mileage record types
export type {
  MileageRecord,
  MileageWindow,
  MileageForecast,
  MileageProjection,
  MileageVariance,
  MileageWarning,
  FleetMileageDistribution,
  MileageBand,
  DailyFleetMileageSummary,
  MileageThresholdConfig,
} from './mileage';

export {
  DEFAULT_MILEAGE_THRESHOLDS,
  DEFAULT_PM_MILEAGE_THRESHOLDS,
  getMileageWindowStatus,
  getVarianceStatus,
  getWarningSeverity,
} from './mileage';

// Maintenance plan types
export type {
  MaintenanceCategory,
  MaintenanceStatus,
  MaintenanceActivity,
  SparePart,
  MaintenancePlan,
  ScheduledMaintenance,
  ActivityProgress,
  Overhaul,
  MaintenanceCompliance,
  MaintenanceIssue,
  MaintenanceScheduleSummary,
  MaintenanceWindow,
} from './maintenance-plan';

export {
  DEFAULT_MAINTENANCE_PLANS,
  getDefaultMaintenancePlan,
  estimateMaintenanceCost,
  calculateMaintenanceWindow,
} from './maintenance-plan';

// Run assignment types
export type {
  Route,
  RunAssignment,
  DailySchedule,
  TrainRecommendation,
  AssignmentOptimizationRequest,
  AssignmentOptimizationResult,
  AssignmentWarning,
  MileageDeviation,
  FleetDailySummary,
  OpsMaintenanceMessage,
} from './run-assignment';

export {
  DEFAULT_ROUTES,
  calculateTrainScore,
  getMileageDeviationStatus,
} from './run-assignment';

// Resource and cost types
export type {
  ManHourAllocation,
  ManHourConfig,
  WorkshopTrack,
  TrackSlot,
  TrackUtilization,
  SparePart as SparePartResource,
  SparePartConsumption,
  SparePartForecast,
  CostBaseline,
  CostProjection,
  CostCompliance,
  LongTermResourcePlan,
  AnnualResourcePlan,
  ResourceLevelingResult,
} from './resource';

export {
  DEFAULT_MANHOUR_CONFIG,
  DEFAULT_WORKSHOP_TRACKS,
  getUtilizationStatus,
  getCostComplianceStatus,
  calculateLevelingScore,
} from './resource';

// Simulation types
export type {
  MileageStrategy,
  ScenarioParameter,
  Scenario,
  SimulationResult,
  SimulationMetrics,
  SimulationWarning,
  ScenarioComparison,
  SensitivityAnalysis,
  MultiScenarioAnalysis,
} from './simulation';

export {
  DEFAULT_SCENARIO_PARAMETERS,
  PRESET_SCENARIOS,
  calculateOverallScore,
  dominates,
} from './simulation';

// Man-Hour Projection Chart types (RFQ Section 8)
export type {
  ChartPeriod,
  ManHourDataPoint,
  GapSeverity,
  ComparisonMode,
  FleetFilter,
  TimeRange,
  ManHourChartConfig,
  ManHourChartState,
  ChartTooltipData,
  QuickRange,
  ChartDimensions,
  DataTableColumn,
  ChartEvents,
  ManHourSummary,
} from './man-hour-chart';

export {
  GAP_THRESHOLDS,
  classifyGap,
  getGapColor,
  DEFAULT_CHART_CONFIG,
  INITIAL_CHART_STATE,
  QUICK_RANGE_MONTHS,
  CHART_COLORS,
  CHART_DIMENSIONS,
  DEFAULT_TABLE_COLUMNS,
  formatPeriodLabel,
  calculateUtilization,
  createEmptyDataPoint,
  generatePeriods,
  calculateSummary,
} from './man-hour-chart';

// Dashboard types (RFQ Section 5 - Centralised Dashboard)
export type {
  KPIState,
  TrendDirection,
  KPIMetricId,
  KPICardData,
  KPIThresholds,
  FleetStatusSummary,
  TrainStatusRow,
  AlertSeverity,
  AlertCategory,
  DashboardAlert,
  AlertAction,
  PMTimelineMarker,
  TimelineConfig,
  AssignmentStatus,
  RunAssignmentRow,
  DailyAssignmentSummary,
  DashboardRole,
  DashboardViewMode,
  WidgetId,
  WidgetConfig,
  DashboardLayout,
  DashboardState,
} from './dashboard';

export {
  ALERT_STYLES,
  PM_LEVEL_COLORS,
  DASHBOARD_COLORS,
  BREAKPOINTS,
  GRID_CONFIG,
  getKPIState,
  formatKPIValue,
  getTrendDirection,
  calculateStatusPercentages,
  getDefaultWidgetsForRole,
} from './dashboard';

// Train Mileage Grid types (MILEAGE-UX-004)
export type {
  TrainGridColumnId,
  ColumnFilterType,
  CellType,
  StickyPosition,
  TrainGridColumn,
  PMWindowStatus,
  PMWindowData,
  FleetFilterOption,
  StatusFilterOption,
  PMDueFilterOption,
  TrainGridFilters,
  FilterChip,
  SortDirection,
  ColumnSort,
  PageSizeOption,
  PaginationState,
  SelectionState,
  BulkActionType,
  BulkAction,
  RowActionType,
  RowAction,
  TrainGridRow,
  RowState,
  FleetSummaryCounts,
  TrainGridState,
} from './train-grid';

export {
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_GRID_COLUMNS,
  PM_WINDOW_THRESHOLDS,
  PM_WINDOW_COLORS,
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_GRID_FILTERS,
  DEFAULT_SORT,
  DEFAULT_PAGINATION,
  BULK_ACTIONS,
  ROW_ACTIONS,
  ROW_STYLES,
  getPMWindowStatus,
  calculatePMWindow,
  formatMileage,
  formatDaysRemaining,
  getFilterChips,
  calculateSummaryCounts,
} from './train-grid';

// Run Assignment UI types (MILEAGE-UX-005)
export type {
  RunAssignmentView,
  ScheduleStatus,
  FleetOverviewData,
  FleetStatusBreakdown,
  TrainPoolFilters,
  TrainPoolSortField,
  TrainPoolSortDirection,
  TrainPoolSort,
  TrainPoolCard,
  RouteSlotState,
  RouteSlot,
  DragTrainItem,
  DropResult,
  AISuggestionState,
  AISuggestion,
  ScoreFactor,
  ScoreBreakdown,
  BulkAcceptSummary,
  BalanceScoreLevel,
  FleetMileageBalance,
  DailyMileageBalance,
  WeeklyCellState,
  WeeklyGridCell,
  WeeklyPlannerData,
  TrainWeekView,
  TrainDayStatus,
  MessageFilters,
  MessageDraft,
  ToastType,
  ToastNotification,
  DailyScheduleViewState,
  WeeklyPlannerViewState,
  MessagesViewState,
  RunAssignmentPageState,
  AssignmentActionType,
  AssignmentAction,
  UndoEntry,
  OverrideConfirmation,
  ConflictType,
  AssignmentConflict,
  ScoreRange,
} from './run-assignment-ui';

export {
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_POOL_FILTERS,
  DEFAULT_TRAIN_POOL_SORT,
  BALANCE_SCORE_THRESHOLDS,
  BALANCE_SCORE_COLORS,
  getBalanceScoreLevel,
  DEFAULT_MESSAGE_FILTERS,
  TOAST_STYLES,
  SCORE_RANGE_THRESHOLDS,
  SCORE_RANGE_COLORS,
  getScoreRange,
  getScoreColor,
  PRIORITY_COLORS,

// Mobile Responsive UI types (MILEAGE-UX-010)

content.text:
Perfect! I've now comprehensively explored all the UX specifications and TypeScript types. Let me provide you with a complete structured summary.

---

## ROLLING STOCK MILEAGE MANAGEMENT - COMPLETE UX SPECIFICATIONS & COMPONENT REQUIREMENTS

### OVERVIEW
The system manages **128 trains** (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) over a **12-year contract period** with comprehensive frontend UI across 9 major screens plus mobile responsiveness.

---

## 1. CENTRALISED DASHBOARD (MILEAGE-UX-003)

### Purpose
Primary entry point for all users with role-based customization and real-time fleet overview.

### Key Components Needed

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **KPI Cards (4)** | Metric Display | Fleet availability %, PM compliance %, Resource util %, Cost variance % | Click вҶ’ drill-down to detail view |
| **Fleet Status Panel** | Status Summary | Train counts by fitness, PM summary | Filter by fleet, view distribution |
| **Alerts & Warnings** | Alert List | Critical/warning/info severity alerts | Dismiss, take action, expand details |
| **PM Schedule Timeline** | Timeline View | 30+ days of PM events with dates | Hover for tooltips, click to detail |
| **Resource Utilisation Chart** | Line/Area Chart | Man-hours available vs required | Hover for tooltip, show gap severity |
| **Today's Run Assignments** | Table/Grid | Route, train, fitness, km, status | Click row for details, filter |
| **Quick Actions** | Button Group | Links to schedule PM, view reports | Navigate to related sections |

### Visualizations
- **4-column KPI card layout** with trend indicators (вҶ‘вҶ“вҶ’)
- **Progress bars** for fleet distribution (FIT/MAINT/OOS)
- **Stacked area chart** with 3 lines (available, adjusted workload, predicted)
- **Timeline with dots** for PM events (в—Ҹв—Ӣв—җвҠ— for different statuses)
- **Color-coded** status indicators (green/yellow/red/orange)

### Responsive Breakpoints
- **Desktop (вүҘ1440px)**: Full 4-column KPI, side-by-side panels
- **Tablet (768-1023px)**: 2-column KPI, stacked panels
- **Mobile (<768px)**: Single column, swipe carousel for KPIs

---

## 2. TRAIN MILEAGE STATUS GRID (MILEAGE-UX-004)

### Purpose
Data-dense, scannable view of all 128 trains with real-time mileage and PM window status.

### Key Components Needed

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Filter Bar** | Control Group | Fleet, Status, PM Due, Search | Real-time filtering, debounced search |
| **Summary Bar** | Metric Display | Total, FIT count, Maint count, OOS count, PM due this week | Quick overview |
| **Data Grid** | Table/Virtual List | 12 columns, 128 rows, sticky header | Sort, filter, select, paginate |
| **PM Window Cell** | Custom Cell | Progress bar + percentage + remaining km | Visual status indication |
| **Status Chips** | Badge Component | Fitness status + Train status | Color-coded (green/amber/red) |
| **Bulk Actions Bar** | Action Toolbar | Schedule PM, Export, Clear | Multi-select support |
| **Pagination** | Control | 25/50/100/all options | Jump to page, keyboard support |

### Visualizations
- **Progress bars** in PM window column (0-100% with color thresholds)
- **Status chips** with icons (вң“вҡ вң• for fitness)
- **Alternating row colors** (zebra striping)
- **Row highlight on hover/selection**

### Virtual Scrolling
- Render only visible rows (128 trains total)
- Smooth 60fps scrolling
- 52px row height

---

## 3. MAN-HOUR PROJECTION CHART (MILEAGE-UX-002)

### Purpose
Core resource planning visualization showing workload vs capacity over 12 years (RFQ Section 8).

### Key Components Needed

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Stacked Area Chart** | Data Visualization | Availability (area), Adjusted workload (line), Predicted workload (dashed) | Hover for tooltip, click for details |
| **Data Table Below** | Table | Period, tasks, man-hours, gap severity, utilization | Sort, filter, export |
| **Timeline Controls** | Control Group | Start/end month pickers, quick ranges (1M/3M/6M/1Y/ALL) | Date range selection |
| **Comparison Toggle** | Button Group | Without/With adjustment, Side-by-side mode | Mode switching |
| **Gap Row Highlighting** | Visual Indicator | Color-coded gaps (green/yellow/orange/red) | Visual severity |
| **KPI Summary** | Metric Display | Total workload, peak utilization, critical periods | Quick metrics |

### Visualizations
- **3-series area/line chart** with color overlays
- **Gap zones** (green/red shading for over/under allocation)
- **Hover tooltip** with full data point details
- **Data table** with 7 columns (Timeline, Tasks, Maint Work, Adj Work, Available, Manpower, Gap)

### Data Requirements
- 144 data points (12 years Г— 12 months)
- Breakdown by fleet type (Kinki Sharyo, Alstom)
- Gap severity classification

---

## 4. RUN ASSIGNMENT UI (MILEAGE-UX-005)

### Purpose
AI-assisted daily train-to-route assignment with mileage balancing and recommendation transparency.

### Key Components Needed

**Daily Schedule View (Primary)**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Fleet Overview Panel** | Summary Cards | In-service, In-maint, OOS, Reserved counts | Filter train pool |
| **Train Pool** | Draggable List | 72 available trains with fitness, mileage, PM info | Drag to assignment slot, filter/sort |
| **Route Assignment Board** | Drag-Drop Board | Routes as slots, assigned trains, AI suggestions | Drag train to route, accept/reject AI |
| **AI Recommendation Card** | Modal/Drawer | Score, breakdown, alternatives, reasoning | Accept, reject, view alternatives |
| **Mileage Balance Chart** | Progress Bar | Kinki vs Alstom actual vs target | Visual balance indicator |
| **Warnings Panel** | Alert List | PM approaching, low reserve, resource gaps | Priority indicators |

**Weekly Planner View (Secondary)**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **7-Day Grid** | Calendar Grid | Route Г— Day assignments for 7 days | Click cell to assign |
| **Train Continuity View** | Visual Timeline | Individual train schedule across week | Highlight conflicts |

**Messaging (Operations-Maintenance)**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Message Panel** | List | Inbox/outbox messages with priority | Reply, acknowledge, filter |
| **Compose Modal** | Form | Train selector, message template, recipient | Send message |

### Visualizations
- **Train cards** with fitness indicator + mileage + PM countdown
- **Drag-and-drop visual feedback** (blue outline on valid drop, red on invalid)
- **Score breakdown** as horizontal stacked bars
- **Mileage balance** as two progress bars (KS + AL)

### AI Recommendations
- Score: 0-100 with factor breakdown (fitness 30%, mileage 40%, PM distance 30%)
- Transparency: Show "why this train" reasoning
- Alternatives: List top 3 options with trade-off explanation

---

## 5. MAINTENANCE COMPLIANCE DASHBOARD (MILEAGE-UX-006)

### Purpose
Monitor PM schedule adherence, cost compliance, and mileage variance across 12-year contract.

### Key Components Needed

**Executive Summary (Default)**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **KPI Cards (4)** | Metric Display | Overall compliance, PM compliance, Cost compliance, Mileage balance | Navigate to detail view |
| **Compliance Trend** | Line Chart | 12-month trend with target band | Hover for monthly detail |
| **Compliance Breakdown** | Bar Chart | Schedule/Cost/Mileage/Resource % stacked | View by category |
| **Fleet Comparison** | Side-by-Side | KS vs AL compliance metrics | Filter by fleet |
| **Alerts Summary** | Alert List | Critical/warning alerts (5 items) | Click to expand view |

**Schedule Compliance Tab**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **PM Compliance Gauge** | Radial Chart | 91% actual vs 90% target | Hover for detail |
| **Compliance by PM Level** | Stacked Bar | PM1/PM2/PM3/Overhaul completion % | Compare levels |
| **Backlog Status** | Trend Chart | Aging backlog over time | View composition |
| **Overdue Analysis** | Table | Train ID, Fleet, Level, Days Late, Mileage | Sort, filter |

**Cost Compliance Tab**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Budget vs Actual Chart** | Side-by-side Bar | Monthly YTD comparison | Drill into categories |
| **Variance Waterfall** | Waterfall Chart | Budget вҶ’ Variances вҶ’ Actual | Click segment for detail |
| **Category Breakdown** | Table | Material/Labor/Service/Overhead variance % | Filter by category |
| **Fleet Cost Comparison** | Dual Chart | KS vs AL cost per train, trend | Compare fleets |

**Mileage Compliance Tab**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Planned vs Actual Chart** | Line Chart | 12-month projection vs actual with Вұ5% band | Hover for detail |
| **Fleet Balance Chart** | Stacked Bar | KS actual vs target, AL actual vs target | View recommendation |
| **Deviation Heatmap** | Grid | 128 trains Г— deviation severity (color) | Click train for history |

**Reports Tab**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Report Library** | Card Grid | Standard reports with preview | Generate, schedule, export |
| **Scheduled Reports** | List | Frequency, next run, recipients | Pause, resume, delete |
| **Custom Report Builder** | Form + Canvas | Drag components, data binding, formatting | Save, generate, preview |

---

## 6. WHAT-IF SCENARIO / SIMULATION (MILEAGE-UX-007)

### Purpose
Model different mileage strategies with 12-year projection impact analysis.

### Key Components Needed

**Scenario Manager**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Quick Start Templates** | Card Grid | 6 presets (Aggressive/Conservative/Balanced/Cost/Seasonal/Balance) | Create from template |
| **Scenario List** | Table | Name, strategy, status, created date | Edit, clone, compare, delete |
| **Recent Comparisons** | Link Cards | Previous comparisons with results | Load comparison |

**Scenario Builder**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Parameter Sliders** | Slider Group | PM buffer (0-25%), Mileage tolerance (-10% to +15%), Fleet balance (70-100%), Man-hours, Workshop tracks, Overtime | Real-time preview |
| **Simulation Preview Panel** | Metric Display | Projected outcomes (KPI deltas), Fleet impact, Risk indicators | Live update as sliders change |
| **Advanced Settings** | Collapsible | Horizon (3m-full), Start/end dates | Customize timeframe |

**Scenario Comparison**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Side-by-Side View** | Card Grid | 2-4 scenarios with KPI cards, delta indicators | View overlay or table |
| **Overlay View** | Line Chart | Timeline projections for each scenario | Compare trends |
| **Comparison Table** | Table | Rows = metrics, cols = scenarios with ranking | Sort by column |
| **Sensitivity Analysis** | Tornado Chart | Parameter impact on outcomes (cost/KPIs) | Identify high-impact variables |
| **Monte Carlo Distribution** | Distribution Chart | Probability curve of outcomes (1000 simulations) | Visualize uncertainty |

### Visualizations
- **Slider feedback**: Live KPI cards update (<100ms response)
- **Delta indicators**: рҹҹў (better), рҹҹЎ (acceptable), рҹ”ҙ (worse)
- **Comparison heat map**: Green for best, red for worst per row
- **Tornado chart**: Bar width shows Вұrange impact

---

## 7. REPORT GENERATION (MILEAGE-UX-008)

### Purpose
Generate, schedule, customize, and export maintenance, mileage, and compliance reports.

### Key Components Needed

**Standard Report Library**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Report Cards** | Card Grid | Icon, name, description, formats, last generated | Generate, schedule, preview |
| **Frequently Used** | Carousel | Top 3 reports with quick access | One-click generate |
| **Report Categories** | Tabs | Maintenance / Mileage / Cost / Operations / Compliance / Executive | Filter by category |

**Generation Flow**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Period Selector** | Control Group | Month/Quarter/YTD/Custom date range | Select period |
| **Fleet & Level Selection** | Checkbox Group | Kinki Sharyo, Alstom, PM levels | Multi-select |
| **Detail Options** | Checkbox Group | Train breakdown, trend charts, notes | Customize content |
| **Format & Export** | Dropdown | PDF/Excel/CSV/HTML | Choose output format |
| **Preview Modal** | Viewer | Embedded PDF preview or table view | Scroll, navigate pages |
| **Progress Bar** | Progress | Generating X of 128 trains (large reports) | Show status |

**Scheduled Reports**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Schedule List** | Table | Report, frequency, next run, recipients, status | Pause, resume, edit, delete |
| **Schedule Editor** | Modal/Form | Frequency (daily/weekly/monthly), time, recipients, archive options | Configure schedule |
| **Distribution Config** | Form | Email recipients, attachment options, archive retention | Setup distribution |

**Custom Report Builder**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Data Sources Panel** | Sidebar | Available datasets (Trains, Mileage, Maintenance, Costs) | Drag to canvas |
| **Component Library** | Sidebar | Header, Text, KPI Card, Table, Chart, Summary, Page Break, Image | Drag to canvas |
| **Report Canvas** | Main Area | WYSIWYG editor with components | Arrange, resize, configure |
| **Properties Panel** | Sidebar | Component settings, data binding, formatting | Configure selected component |

### Export Formats
- **PDF**: Branded, page numbers, signatures, pixel-perfect layout
- **Excel**: Formulas, pivot tables, conditional formatting, auto-filters
- **CSV**: Raw data for external analysis
- **HTML**: Email-friendly, responsive
- **JSON**: System integration

---

## 8. ALERT & NOTIFICATION SYSTEM (MILEAGE-UX-009)

### Purpose
Comprehensive alert management with escalation, delivery channels, and user customization.

### Key Components Needed

**Notification Types**

| Type | Position | Duration | Max Visible | Use Case |
|------|----------|----------|------------|----------|
| **Toast** | Top-right | 5s auto-dismiss | 3 stacked | Confirmations, simple feedback |
| **Inline** | Content top | Persistent | N/A | Page-specific alerts |
| **Actionable** | Page top | Until action | 1 blocking | Critical decisions |
| **Modal** | Center overlay | Until close | 1 blocking | Blocking confirmations |

**Notification Center**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Bell Icon Badge** | Badge | Unread count (0-99+) with pulse if critical | Navigate to center |
| **Panel (Drop-down)** | Drawer | List of recent notifications by date | Mark read, dismiss, action |
| **Full Page View** | Table/List | Filters (category, priority, status), search, pagination | Filter, sort, bulk actions |
| **Notification Item** | Card | Icon, title, message, time, action buttons | Expand, take action |

**Escalation Workflow**

| Tier | Delay | Audience | Channels | Trigger |
|------|-------|----------|----------|---------|
| 1 | 15 min | Assigned user/team | In-app, Push, Email | Alert created |
| 2 | 30 min | Supervisor | All + SMS | Tier 1 unacknowledged |
| 3 | 1 hr | Manager | All channels | Tier 2 unacknowledged |
| 4 | Tracked | Executive | All + Phone | Tier 3 unacknowledged |

**User Preferences**

| Component | Type | Data Requirements | Interactions |
|-----------|------|-------------------|--------------|
| **Channel Toggles** | Toggle Group | In-app, Push, Email, SMS enable/disable | Enable/disable channels |
| **Quiet Hours** | Time Range | Start/end time with critical exception | Configure quiet window |
| **Daily Digest** | Toggle + Time | Aggregate warnings/info into 1 email | Set delivery time |
| **Category Preferences** | Table | Per-category channel matrix | Configure per category |
| **Frequency Limits** | Slider | Max notifications per hour, bundling | Prevent alert fatigue |
| **Subscriptions** | Checkbox List | Fleet, train, route subscriptions with alert types | Subscribe/unsubscribe |

### Alert Categories & Priorities

| Category | P1 Critical | P2 Warning | P3 Info | P4 Success |
|----------|------------|-----------|---------|-----------|
| **Maintenance** | PM Overdue | PM Approaching | PM Scheduled | PM Completed |
| **Mileage** | N/A | Overrun/Underrun | Forecast conflict | N/A |
| **Assignment** | No suitable train | Conflict detected | Schedule not published | Assigned |
| **Resource** | Man-hour shortage | Near capacity | Overtime required | N/A |
| **Cost** | Budget exceeded | Budget warning | Variance >5% | N/A |
| **System** | Integration failure | Sync delayed | Report failed | N/A |

### Visualization
- **Status colors**: рҹ”ҙP1 / рҹҹЎP2 / рҹ”өP3 / рҹҹўP4
- **Toast animations**: Slide in from right (300ms), slide out (200ms)
- **Badge pulse**: Continuous animation for critical alerts
- **Priority icon + text**: Never color-only indication

---

## 9. MOBILE RESPONSIVE DESIGN (MILEAGE-UX-010)

### Purpose
Field-accessible mobile-first design with offline-first capabilities for Operations, Maintenance, and Management roles.

### Responsive Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout | Navigation |
|-----------|-------|--------|-----------|
| **xs (Mobile)** | <576px | Single column | Bottom tab bar (5 max) |
| **sm (Mobile Large)** | 576-767px | Single column | Bottom tab bar |
| **md (Tablet Portrait)** | 768-991px | 2-column | Horizontal tabs + hamburger |
| **lg (Tablet Landscape)** | 992-1199px | 3-column | Horizontal tabs |
| **xl (Desktop)** | 1200-1399px | Full layout | Top navigation |
| **xxl (Desktop Large)** | вүҘ1400px | Full layout | Top navigation |

### Navigation Patterns

**Mobile Bottom Tab Bar (Primary)**
```
Home | Trains | Schedule | Alerts | More
```

**Mobile Hamburger Menu (Secondary)**
```
[Profile] вҶ’ Dashboard, Trains, Schedule, Compliance, Reports, Analytics, Settings, Help, Logout
```

**Tablet Horizontal Tabs + Condensed**
```
[вҳ°] [Logo] Dashboard | Trains | Schedule | Compliance | Reports [рҹ””] [рҹ‘Ө]
```

### Key Components Needed (Mobile)

| Component | Type | Adaptation | Interaction |
|-----------|------|-----------|------------|
| **Card-Based Dashboard** | Layout | Replace tables with cards | Tap for details |
| **KPI Carousel** | Horizontal Scroll | 4 KPIs in swipeable carousel | Swipe left/right |
| **Collapsible Sections** | Expandable | PM Status, Mileage, Alerts collapse | Tap to expand |
| **Train Grid Cards** | Card List | Full table вҶ’ compact cards | Tap for full details |
| **Filter Bottom Sheet** | Modal Drawer | Slides from bottom with snap points | Drag to dismiss |
| **Date Picker Native** | Input | Use system date picker `<input type="date">` | Mobile-friendly |
| **Action Buttons** | Icon Buttons | Icons with labels on long-press | Touch-friendly 48x48px |
| **Sparkline Charts** | Simplified | Small inline charts, tap to expand | Expand to full chart |

### Offline-First Architecture

| Feature | Offline Support | Sync Method |
|---------|-----------------|------------|
| **View Train List** | вң“ Full (cached) | WebSocket updates when online |
| **Record Mileage** | вң“ Queue | Queue for background sync |
| **View PM Schedule** | вң“ Full (cached) | Stale-while-revalidate |
| **Create Assignment** | вң“ Queue | Sync when online |
| **View Notifications** | вң“ Recent only | Pull when online |
| **Generate Reports** | вң— No | Server-only |
| **Run Simulations** | вң— No | Server-only |

### Offline Indicators
```
вҡЎ Offline - Changes will sync when connected
KS-045 вҸі Syncing...  (with retry option)
KS-046 вң“ Synced      (confirmation)
```

### PWA Features
- **Install Prompt**: "Add to Home Screen" modal
- **Service Worker**: Cache-first for shell, stale-while-revalidate for data
- **Manifest**: Icon sets (192px, 512px), theme colors, orientation
- **Push Notifications**: FCM/APNs for P1 critical alerts
- **Background Sync**: Queue for mileage, assignments when offline

### Touch Optimization
- **Minimum touch target**: 48x48px (comfortable: 56px)
- **Swipe gestures**: Swipe right for done, left for delete
- **Pull-to-refresh**: Refresh data with pull gesture
- **Haptic feedback**: Vibration on critical alerts
- **High contrast mode**: For outdoor/glove readiness

### Field Worker Optimizations
- **Quick check-in flow**: QR code scan or manual train ID entry
- **Large buttons**: 64px+ for gloved operation
- **Voice input**: Record mileage by voice with confirmation
- **Simple workflows**: 2-3 taps maximum for common actions
- **Progress indicators**: Clear status for async operations

## SUMMARY: COMPONENT INVENTORY

### Atomic Components
- **KPI Card**: Value + trend + threshold state
- **Status Chip**: Fitness/train status with icon
- **Progress Bar**: PM window with color zones
- **Alert Card**: Severity + action buttons
- **Toast**: Auto-dismiss notification
- **Badge**: Unread count with pulse animation
- **Expandable Section**: Collapsible header + content
- **Filter Pill**: Removable filter indicator

### Composite Components
- **KPI Dashboard**: 4-column grid of KPI cards
- **Fleet Grid**: Table with 128 trains, sorting, filtering, pagination
- **Timeline Chart**: PM schedule with date range and zoom
- **Line/Area Chart**: Man-hours availability + workload + gap zones
- **Drag-Drop Board**: Routes Г— trains with AI recommendations
- **Comparison Panel**: Side-by-side scenario metrics
- **Report Preview**: Embedded PDF viewer
- **Notification Center**: List + full page view

### Complex Features
- **Virtual Scrolling**: 128 trains, smooth 60fps
- **Real-time WebSocket**: Live fleet status updates
- **Offline Queue**: Mileage/assignment queue with retry
- **Background Sync**: Silent sync when online
- **Service Worker**: App shell + data caching
- **Push Notifications**: Multi-channel delivery
- **Drag-and-Drop**: Visual feedback + accessibility
- **Responsive Grids**: CSS Grid with breakpoint rules

---

## DATA STRUCTURE HIGHLIGHTS

All components feed from TypeScript types supporting:

- **128 trains** identified as `{fleetType}-{number}` (KS-001 to KS-049, AL-001 to AL-079)
- **12 PM levels**: PM1/PM2/PM3/OVERHAUL + mileage thresholds
- **Fitness statuses**: FIT/RESTRICTED/UNFIT
- **Train statuses**: IN_SERVICE/IN_MAINTENANCE/OUT_OF_SERVICE/RESERVED
- **144 data points** (12 years Г— 12 months) for projections
- **Real-time metrics**: Availability %, compliance %, utilization %
- **Multi-level aggregation**: Monthly/quarterly/yearly views

This comprehensive UX specification covers 9 major screens + mobile, with detailed component breakdowns, data requirements, interactions, visualizations, and responsive design patterns for building the complete frontend application.
